target.title: feat(fireworks): catalog DeepSeek V4 Pro, MiniMax M2.7, GLM-5.1, GPT-OSS 120B reasoning models
target.body:
**TL;DR:** I run OpenClaw against Fireworks serverless and wanted their current reasoning lineup — DeepSeek V4 Pro, MiniMax M2.7, GLM-5.1, GPT-OSS 120B — selectable like any bundled model instead of falling through to the dynamic-model guesswork. Cataloging DeepSeek V4 on Fireworks also trips a real bug: core's deepseek-native fallback injects `thinking` next to `reasoning_effort` and Fireworks rejects that request outright. The fix turned out to need zero new runtime code — one manifest compat field core already supports. ## Summary **What does this add?** Four Fireworks serverless reasoning models to the bundled `extensions/fireworks` manifest catalog, each with `reasoning_effort` compat (supported efforts + level map) and a per-family thinking profile so thinking menus only offer levels the deployment actually accepts: | model | thinking levels | default | context | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | `deepseek-v4-pro` | off…max | high | 1,048,576 | | `minimax-m2p7` | low/medium/high (no off) | medium | 196,608 | | `glm-5p1` | binary off/on | on | 202,752 | | `gpt-oss-120b` | low/medium/high | low | 131,072 | **What does this fix?** Without the patch, selecting `fireworks/accounts/fireworks/models/deepseek-v4-pro` with thinking enabled kills every turn. Core's DeepSeek V4 fallback (`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/extra-params.ts`) matches the id's last segment (`deepseek-v4-pro`) on any unowned OpenAI-compatible provider and injects `thinking: { type: "enabled" }` plus `reasoning_effort`. Fireworks rejects that payload: ``` {"error":{...,"message":"cannot specify both 'thinking' and 'reasoning_effort'"}} ``` **Why is one manifest field the right fix?** Core already built the seam for exactly this class of deployment: `compat.thinkingFormat` ≠ `"deepseek"` suppresses the injected `thinking` entirely (even `{type:"disabled"}`) and strips deepseek replay fields, while `reasoning_effort` keeps flowing from the manifest map. The doc comment on `deepSeekV4NativeThinkingAllowedByCompat` names the use case (Azure AI Foundry DeepSeek V4 rejects `thinking` outright), and `extra-params.deepseek-v4-thinking-format.test.ts` pins the whole behavior. So the deepseek-v4-pro row carries `"thinkingFormat": "openai"` and no stream wrapper is added (see review-state item 1 — an earlier iteration had one). **Thinking `off` no longer escalates to max.** MiniMax M2.7 and GPT-OSS 120B are the first bundled profiles without an `off` level, and they exposed a core clamp quirk: when every supported level exceeds the request, `resolveSupportedThinkingLevelFromProfile` picked the *highest* level — so switching a thinking-off session onto these models would silently land on maximum reasoning spend. One-line core fix (`src/auto-reply/thinking.ts`): clamp down to the lowest non-off level instead, with a regression test. **Dynamic ids too, with empirical red→green.** The catalog rows alone are not enough, for two verified reasons: (1) non-cataloged `deepseek-v4*` ids still hit core's id-family matcher — the 400 reproduced live via `openclaw agent --local` on the manifest-only iteration, green with the fix; (2) Fireworks' baseUrl classifies as a proxy-like endpoint (`resolveProviderEndpoint` → `custom`, probed on this branch), so detected transport defaults disable `reasoning_effort` and a dynamic id showing a thinking menu would never encode the chosen level. `resolveFireworksDynamicModel` therefore merges each profile-matched family's manifest-row compat (single source of truth) into dynamic ids via `applyModelCompatPatch`, keeping menus and wire behavior 1:1 for all four families. **Where do the numbers come from?** Model ids and context windows verified against the live Fireworks API (`/v1/models`; `minimax-m2p7` is absent from that account-scoped listing, so: live 1-token completion + model page). Pricing incl. cache-read rates verified against `https://docs.fireworks.ai/serverless/pricing`. ## Linked context - #90326 (open, same author) resolves catalog model limits from `openclaw.plugin.json` via core and refactors the same plugin files — complementary companion; this PR's live verification ran without it. Whichever lands second needs a textual rebase (both touch `model-id.ts`/`provider-catalog.ts`). - Core seam + tests: `extra-params.ts` (`deepSeekV4NativeThinkingAllowedByCompat`, non-native sanitizer), `extra-params.deepseek-v4-thinking-format.test.ts`. - Sibling precedent: `extensions/deepseek/openclaw.plugin.json` ships the same `supportsReasoningEffort` compat; `deepseek-v4-pro` pricing matches the first-party row. - Manifest compat keys (`thinkingFormat`, `supportedReasoningEfforts`, `reasoningEffortMap`) are existing config schema: `src/config/zod-schema.core.ts`, `src/config/types.models.ts`. ## Real behavior proof Behavior or issue addressed: Fireworks DeepSeek V4 Pro turns failing with 400 "cannot specify both 'thinking' and 'reasoning_effort'" when thinking is enabled; the three other reasoning families missing from the bundled catalog (no pricing, wrong default thinking levels via dynamic fallback). Real environment tested: live Fireworks serverless API (`api.fireworks.ai/inference/v1`) with a real API key; OpenClaw checkout of this branch on Linux/Node 24. Exact steps or command run after this patch: `openclaw models list --all --provider fireworks --plain`, then one noninteractive agent turn per model, e.g. `openclaw agent --session-id fw-shot-ds -m "One sentence: why is the sky blue?" --model fireworks/accounts/fireworks/models/deepseek-v4-pro --thinking high` (and the minimax/glm/gpt-oss equivalents). Evidence after fix: screenshot below; red→green at the same command: `openclaw agent --local --session-id fw-proof-ds -m "Say READY and nothing else." --model fireworks/accounts/fireworks/models/deepseek-v4-pro --thinking high` → before: `error=LLM request failed … rawError=400 cannot specify both 'thinking' and 'reasoning_effort'`; after: `READY`. Raw API contract probe: a direct `POST /v1/chat/completions` for `deepseek-v4-pro` with `{"thinking":{"type":"enabled"},"reasoning_effort":"low"}` returns the same 400, while `{"reasoning_effort":"low"}` alone returns a normal completion — the patched plugin emits the latter shape. Observed result after fix: all four models listed in the catalog with pricing; live one-turn agent runs on this branch return model replies for all four families (deepseek-v4-pro/minimax-m2p7/glm-5p1/gpt-oss-120b), including a deepseek-v4-pro turn at `--thinking max` through the dynamic-id path. What was not tested: Fire Pass / `routers/*-fast` ids (separate follow-up), image input (all four rows are text-only per the live API), `xhigh`/`max` efforts end-to-end on deepseek (mapped per Fireworks' documented effort list but not exercised in a live turn). ## Tests and validation - `extensions/fireworks` vitest: 18 passed (catalog compat incl. the new `thinkingFormat` assertion, per-family thinking profiles, dynamic family compat merge, dynamic-model fallbacks); `src/auto-reply/thinking.test.ts`: 48 passed incl. the new no-off clamp regression; clamp consumers (`agent-command.live-model-switch`, cron model-override forwarding): green. - `docs/providers/fireworks.md`: built-in catalog table and dynamic-id thinking notes refreshed with the new models. - Core seam test: `extra-params.deepseek-v4-thinking-format.test.ts` 8 passed (pre-existing; proves the manifest override path this PR relies on). - `tsgo` core + extension lanes clean; oxlint clean; oxfmt clean. - Autoreview loop (claude-fable-5 thinking-high + codex gpt-5.5 medium), nine rounds until convergence; final verdicts: claude "patch is correct (0.85)", codex "patch is correct (0.83), no actionable findings". The full finding-by-finding ledger is in "Current review state" below. ## Risk checklist - Mostly additive manifest rows; plugin code additions (`model-id.ts`/`thinking-policy.ts`/dynamic resolver) are plugin-local and extend the existing kimi pattern. The only core code change is the 4-line clamp-direction fix in `src/auto-reply/thinking.ts`. - No config/default surface changes outside the plugin manifest; no migrations needed; existing configured models are unaffected (catalog rows are additive, ids unchanged). - Dynamic (non-cataloged) Fireworks ids keep the exact pre-PR behavior except `deepseek-v4*`, which gains the compat opt-out (those turns 400'd before, so nothing working changes); `glm-5p1` moves from dynamic guesswork to a manifest row, which only adds pricing/compat it previously lacked. - Thinking-profile levels are clamped to what Fireworks accepts per family, so a user-selected unsupported level can no longer produce a rejected request. The GLM profile applies to GLM-5+ ids only (the verified family); dynamic GLM-4.x ids keep the generic menu. - Core clamp change is strictly cost-reducing: below-range requests now resolve to the lowest supported level instead of the highest; no existing test pinned the old escalate-up behavior. ## Current review state What is the next action? Maintainer review. Author attaches the terminal screenshot (catalog listing + one live turn per family) at the placeholder above. What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? Author: screenshot. Maintainer: review/land decision + ordering vs #90326. CI: standard checks. No external proof outstanding. Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? None posted on this PR yet. Pre-publication, a nine-round autoreview loop (claude-fable-5 thinking-high, then codex gpt-5.5 medium, fix-and-rerun) reviewed every iteration. That loop is why an "add models" commit carries extra fixes — each item below was a reviewer finding against the model rows themselves, folded in before opening the PR: 1. **Deleted the original 75-line DeepSeek stream wrapper** (+ tests) for manifest `thinkingFormat: "openai"` — core's existing tested seam (`extensions/AGENTS.md`: check for a seam before adding a provider-local `wrapStreamFn`). 2. **`docs/providers/fireworks.md` refresh** — docs change with behavior. 3. **Dynamic-id compat for `deepseek-v4*`** — manifest row alone left non-cataloged v4 ids on core's id-family fallback; live 400 reproduced, fixed red→green. 4. **Compat merged via `applyModelCompatPatch`, not replaced** — replacement dropped the template's `unsupportedToolSchemaKeywords`. 5. **Core clamp fix (`src/auto-reply/thinking.ts`)** — first bundled no-off profiles exposed thinking-`off` clamping *up* to the most expensive level; 4 lines + regression test. 6. **GLM matcher narrowed to GLM-5+** — the binary off/on profile is verified only for 5.x; dynamic GLM-4.x ids keep the generic menu. 7. **MiniMax matcher narrowed to m2p7+** — the first-party minimax plugin exposes `off` for M2.x, so no-off is a deployment property, not a family property. 8. **`minimal` dropped from the DeepSeek menu** — it encoded identically to `low`. 9. **Family compat table for all dynamic reasoning ids** — codex P1: the proxy-like endpoint disables detected `reasoning_effort`, so profile-matched dynamic ids would show menus that never encode; confirmed by probe. 10. **`structuredClone` of manifest compat** — consumers must not mutate manifest-derived singletons. 11. **gpt-oss `"off"` map entry dropped** — no-off rows share one convention: omit `off` so a bypassed off request sends no effort. Deliberately not taken (one claude P3): dynamic v4 ids inherit v4-pro's xhigh/max effort enum, which only matters if Fireworks ever ships another serverless v4 variant with a narrower enum — none exists today (`/v1/models` shows v4-pro only).
Review state guidance Keep this as the durable state for review progress. If useful information appears in comments, fold the current next action or blocker back here so maintainers and ClawSweeper do not need to reconstruct state from comment history.

target.title: fix(tools): propagate external content provenance to policy hooks
target.body:
Summary:
- Adds structured external-content provenance to tool-call policy hook types.
- Propagates provenance through HookContext, before_tool_call plugin events, trusted tool policy events, and tool hook context.
- Detects EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT markers from the active embedded run prompt/session context.
- Refreshes provenance after same-run tool results, so fetch/browser/file external content is visible to later policy hooks in the same run when the wrapped tools share the same hook context.
- Documents the public hook field and source semantics.
- Refreshes the plugin SDK type barrel so extension package-boundary cache keys invalidate when hook contract shapes change.

Why:
- External content is already wrapped as untrusted prompt text.
- Before this change, the post-model tool-call policy boundary did not receive structured provenance showing whether external content was present.
- Policy plugins could see the tool name, params, tool kind/input kind, run/toolCallId, derived paths, and session context, but not whether the tool call followed browser, web_fetch, email, webhook, file-fetch, or other untrusted external context.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
Same-run external content provenance reaches the later before_tool_call policy boundary after a web_fetch result introduces wrapped EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT. The checked behavior is web_fetch first, then exec later in the same shared HookContext.

Real environment tested:
Local OpenClaw checkout on branch fix/tool-call-external-provenance at PR head e8800a11833801434e6d48a4a0b3f9b03d06d958, running on Linux with Node v24.15.0 and pnpm 11.2.2. The proof used the real active OpenClaw plugin registry, a real trusted tool policy entry, and the real wrapToolWithBeforeToolCallHook runtime path.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
Ran a live `node --import tsx --eval ...` harness in the PR worktree. The harness registered a trusted tool policy in the active plugin registry, wrapped a web_fetch tool and a bash/exec tool with wrapToolWithBeforeToolCallHook, executed web_fetch first, then executed exec in the same shared hook context.

Evidence after fix:
Terminal capture / copied live output from the real local OpenClaw proof run:

```text
[proof] pr=91800
[proof] head_sha=e8800a11833801434e6d48a4a0b3f9b03d06d958
[proof] using real wrapToolWithBeforeToolCallHook runtime path
[proof] trusted policy count=1
[proof] shared HookContext initially has externalContent=null
[proof-policy] tool=web_fetch toolCallId=proof-web-fetch-call event.externalContent=null ctx.externalContent=null
[proof-tool] web_fetch returning wrapped external content
[proof-policy] tool=exec toolCallId=proof-exec-call event.externalContent={"present":true,"sources":["web_fetch"]} ctx.externalContent={"present":true,"sources":["web_fetch"]}
[proof-tool] exec would run redacted command
[proof-result] first_call=web_fetch externalContent=null status=PASS
[proof-result] second_call=exec event.externalContent={"present":true,"sources":["web_fetch"]} ctx.externalContent={"present":true,"sources":["web_fetch"]} status=PASS
[proof-result] PASS: same-run external web_fetch content reached later before_tool_call policy boundary through wrapped tool execution
```

Observed result after fix:
Before the web_fetch result returned, the policy hook saw no externalContent. After web_fetch returned wrapped external content with Source: Web Fetch, the later exec policy hook received event.externalContent and ctx.externalContent as {"present":true,"sources":["web_fetch"]}. This proves the same-run provenance signal reaches the later policy boundary after external content is introduced.

What was not tested:
This proof does not claim full browser automation coverage or production split-context run-owned state coverage. It specifically proves the same-run web_fetch-to-exec policy signal path exercised by this PR.

## Validation

- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.integration.e2e.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.embedded-mode.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-subpaths.test.ts
- pnpm run test:extensions:package-boundary:compile
- git diff --check

## Local ClawSweeper re-review

Head: `4f2d70681df99881c6d4b48d305e5d2910d3f154`

Result:
- `overallCorrectness`: `patch is correct`
- `reviewFindings`: none
- `securityReview.status`: `cleared`
- `realBehaviorProof.status`: `sufficient`
- Remaining maintainer gate: confirm the additive public plugin hook contract/source taxonomy before merge.

Validation run locally after the repair commit:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts --reporter=dot` -> 58 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.cwd-split.test.ts --reporter=dot` -> 3 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.integration.e2e.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.embedded-mode.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-subpaths.test.ts --reporter=dot` -> 18 + 11 + 19 passed
- `pnpm run test:extensions:package-boundary:compile` -> passed
- `git diff --check` -> passed

target.title: fix(update): preserve local package overrides
target.body:
## Summary

- Preserves local package overrides during package update flows, including standalone added `dist` files, and reports the recovery bundle when overrides are not reapplied.
- Keeps content-inventory validation strict for new tarballs while allowing already-published `2026.6.5` / `2026.6.5-beta.6` packages that predate `dist/postinstall-content-inventory.json`.
- Repairs the `main` merge conflict on `src/cli/update-cli.ts` and `src/infra/update-runner.ts`, then refreshes the branch to current `upstream/main`.
- Adds the missing zh-CN glossary entries needed for the docs check after the latest main merge.

## Verification

- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `pnpm docs:list`
- `PATH=/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH pnpm check:docs`
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD && git diff --check`
- `PATH=/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH ./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/infra/package-local-overrides.ts src/infra/package-update-steps.test.ts src/infra/update-runner.test.ts docs/.i18n/glossary.zh-CN.json`
- `PATH=/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH TMPDIR=/tmp node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/check-openclaw-package-tarball.test.ts test/scripts/postinstall-bundled-plugins.test.ts test/scripts/openclaw-cross-os-release-checks.test.ts src/infra/package-dist-inventory.test.ts src/infra/package-update-steps.test.ts src/infra/update-runner.test.ts src/cli/update-cli/progress.test.ts src/cli/update-cli.option-collisions.test.ts`
- `PATH=/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH TMPDIR=/tmp node --import tsx --input-type=module` current-head local package override smoke
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main --codex-bin /Users/jason/.codex/openclaw-pr-autopilot/bin/codex-persist-sessions --model codex=gpt-5.5 --thinking codex=high` clean after the accepted standalone-added-file finding was fixed.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Package self-update preserves locally modified installed package files by default, captures standalone added `dist` files in the recovery bundle, reapplies trusted overrides only when requested, reports conflicts when upstream changed the same file, and still exposes the preserved recovery bundle when an in-place update command fails after partially mutating the install. The PR also keeps published `2026.6.5` and `2026.6.5-beta.6` tarballs valid even though those already-shipped packages do not contain the new content-inventory file.

Real environment tested: Local macOS source checkout on current PR head `e524238d2dbb3e08bc39c10577960824a04cc11d`, Node `v24.14.0`, pnpm `11.9.0`. The smoke used real filesystem temp package roots and the real `runGlobalPackageUpdateSteps` / `writePackageDistInventory` implementation through `node --import tsx`; it was not a Vitest/mock assertion path. Earlier Blacksmith Testbox smoke remains supplemental for the original branch behavior.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a current-head terminal smoke with `node --import tsx --input-type=module`, creating temporary installed package roots, writing real package dist inventories, mutating installed `dist/index.js` as a local override, adding an unreferenced `dist/local-data.json` file, applying staged update contents, and invoking `runGlobalPackageUpdateSteps` for default preserve, trusted reapply, standalone added-file preserve, conflict preserve, and in-place failure recovery scenarios. Then ran docs check, focused tests, formatter check, `git diff --check`, and nested autoreview on the same head.

Evidence after fix:

```text
CRABBOX_PHASE:local-current-head-smoke
OPENCLAW_UPDATE_LOCAL_OVERRIDES_SMOKE default-preserve {"failedStep":null,"status":"preserved","added":0,"modified":1,"applied":0,"conflicts":[],"installed":"export {};","recovery":"export const local_default_preserve = true;"}
OPENCLAW_UPDATE_LOCAL_OVERRIDES_SMOKE trusted-reapply {"failedStep":null,"status":"applied","added":0,"modified":1,"applied":1,"conflicts":[],"installed":"export const local_trusted_reapply = true;","recovery":"export const local_trusted_reapply = true;"}
OPENCLAW_UPDATE_LOCAL_OVERRIDES_SMOKE standalone-added-preserve {"failedStep":null,"status":"preserved","added":1,"modified":0,"applied":0,"conflicts":[],"installed":"export {};","recovery":null,"addedInstalled":"{\"local\":true}\n","addedRecovery":"{\"local\":true}\n"}
OPENCLAW_UPDATE_LOCAL_OVERRIDES_SMOKE conflict-preserve {"failedStep":null,"status":"conflict","added":0,"modified":1,"applied":0,"conflicts":[{"path":"dist/index.js","reason":"target-changed"}],"installed":"export const upstream = true;","recovery":"export const local_conflict_preserve = true;"}
OPENCLAW_UPDATE_LOCAL_OVERRIDES_SMOKE in-place-failure-recovery {"failedStep":"global update","status":"preserved","added":0,"modified":1,"applied":0,"conflicts":[],"installed":"export const partial_update = true;","recovery":"export const local_failed_update = true;"}
OPENCLAW_UPDATE_LOCAL_OVERRIDES_SMOKE passed
```

Supplemental current-head proof:

```text
Docs formatting clean (662 files).
Summary: 0 error(s)
Docs MDX check passed (677 files, 1861ms).
checked_internal_links=4451
broken_links=0
```

```text
Test Files  8 passed (8)
Tests  270 passed (270)
```

```text
autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported
overall: patch is correct (0.82)
```

Observed result after fix: Default update preserved one local override and left the updated installed file in place while recording the local contents in the recovery bundle. Trusted reapply restored the local override into the installed package. Standalone added-file handling recorded one added file and preserved it in the recovery bundle even without an importer change. Conflict handling kept the upstream-updated file, recorded a `target-changed` conflict, and preserved the local contents for recovery. The in-place failure path reported `failedStep: "global update"` and still emitted `local overrides` status `preserved` with the original local file content in the recovery bundle. Focused tests also covered the content-inventory compatibility cutoff, staged-swap local override preservation, standalone added-file regression, and the in-place failure recovery reporting added during review.

What was not tested: A full `pnpm check` / full repository Vitest run was not rerun after the latest merge and standalone-added-file fix because the touched surface was covered by the focused update, tarball, postinstall, inventory, runner, CLI option/progress tests, docs check, formatter, diff check, current-head real filesystem smoke, and nested autoreview. A fresh remote Crabbox run was not repeated after the merge-conflict repair; the current-head real smoke above is the after-fix behavior proof for this branch.

target.title: fix(gemini): bridge OAuth profiles into CLI runtime
target.body:
## Summary

- opt `google-gemini-cli` into explicit OpenClaw auth-profile forwarding
- refresh/load the selected OAuth profile before CLI backend preparation
- materialize selected Gemini OAuth credentials into an isolated `GEMINI_CLI_HOME/.gemini/oauth_creds.json` for the `gemini` subprocess
- keep Gemini CLI auth epochs profile-owned when the bridge is active

Fixes #88742.

## Proof

HEAD: `4ba89fe8b18f7eef6802752c80bfe8f1359f2b2e`

Validation log: `/tmp/openclaw-pr88742-validation.log`

Commands:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/google/setup-api.test.ts src/agents/cli-backends.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts --reporter=dot
pnpm -s exec oxfmt --check extensions/google/cli-backend.ts extensions/google/setup-api.test.ts src/agents/cli-backends.test.ts src/agents/cli-backends.ts src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.ts src/plugin-sdk/cli-backend.ts src/plugins/cli-backend.types.ts src/plugins/types.ts
pnpm -s exec oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/agents/cli-backends.test.ts src/agents/cli-backends.ts src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.ts src/plugin-sdk/cli-backend.ts src/plugins/cli-backend.types.ts src/plugins/types.ts extensions/google/cli-backend.ts extensions/google/setup-api.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Relevant output:

```text
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  59 passed (59)
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  3 passed (3)
All matched files use the correct format.
```

Source-level behavior proof: `/tmp/openclaw-pr88742-proof.log`

- `extensions/google/setup-api.test.ts` proves selected OAuth credentials produce an isolated Gemini CLI home and writes `.gemini/oauth_creds.json` without logging token material.
- `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts` proves canonical `google/*` models routed through `google-gemini-cli` select the `google-gemini-cli:*` auth profile before `runCliAgent`.

Not tested:

- Full `pnpm -s exec tsgo --noEmit` is blocked on current HEAD by pre-existing errors outside this patch:

```text
src/config/io.ts(192,7): error TS2527: The inferred type of 'configWritePostCommitRollback' references an inaccessible 'unique symbol' type. A type annotation is necessary.
src/secrets/config-io.ts(8,17): error TS4058: Return type of exported function has or is using name 'configWritePostCommitRollback' from external module ".../src/config/io" but cannot be named.
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: `google-gemini-cli` backend preparation receives a selected OpenClaw `google-gemini-cli:*` OAuth profile, materializes that OAuth profile into an isolated `GEMINI_CLI_HOME/.gemini/oauth_creds.json`, and passes that isolated home to the real `gemini` subprocess instead of relying on ambient `~/.gemini` state.
- **Real environment tested**: Local Linux workstation, PR branch `fix/88742-gemini-cli-oauth-bridge`, head `ada77084fb0bce5ccb88ba88b38ed803b5b9f0c6`, OpenClaw `2026.5.31 (7150977)`, `@google/gemini-cli` binary available as `/usr/local/bin/gemini`, real Gemini CLI version `0.40.1`. Token values and account identifiers were not printed.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```text
cd /home/rev/projects/openclaw-prs/pr-88742-gemini-cli-oauth-bridge
pnpm install
pnpm build
confirmed backend registration path:
  rg -n 'buildGoogleGeminiCliBackend|prepareExecution|CliBackendPlugin|cliBackends' src packages extensions/google | head -120
  nl -ba extensions/google/cli-backend.ts | sed -n '1,155p'
created direct backend proof harness:
  .tmp-pr88748-gemini-backend-proof.mts
ran:
  BAD_HOME=/tmp/tmp.6jUzYBOeWy PROFILE='google-gemini-cli:jason.allen.oneal@gmail.com' pnpm exec tsx ./.tmp-pr88748-gemini-backend-proof.mts 2>&1 | tee /tmp/pr88748-direct-backend-proof.log
sanitized:
```

target.title: fix(auto-reply): scope foreground reply fence per inbound message
target.body:
## Summary

When two users concurrently @mention the agent in a group chat with `messages.groupChat.visibleReplies: "automatic"`, both agent runs complete successfully — but only the reply to the latest message is delivered. The reply to the first user is silently cancelled by the foreground reply fence because the fence key was scoped per session target rather than per inbound message.

## Fix

In `src/auto-reply/dispatch.ts`, `resolveForegroundReplyFenceKey` now includes the inbound `MessageSid` in the fence composite key. When available, different inbound messages get independent fences and cannot cancel each other. Falls back to per-target scoping when no inbound message id is present.

**Before:**
```ts
return JSON.stringify([
  "foreground", channel, accountId, sessionKey, chatType, target
]);
```

**After:**
```ts
const inboundMsgId = finalized.MessageSid ?? finalized.MessageSidFirst;
return JSON.stringify([
  "foreground", channel, accountId, sessionKey, chatType, target,
  ...(inboundMsgId ? [inboundMsgId] : [])
]);
```

This means User A's reply (fence key includes MessageSid-A) and User B's reply (fence key includes MessageSid-B) are in separate fences — neither can suppress the other.

## Files changed

- `src/auto-reply/dispatch.ts`: +7 lines

## Related

Fixes #92186

### Real behavior proof
- **Behavior addressed**: Concurrent group @mentions in `automatic` reply mode silently drop all replies except the newest one, because the foreground reply fence scopes per-session-target instead of per-inbound-message
- **Real environment tested**: Linux Node 24
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```bash
  cd /path/to/openclaw
  node ./node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/dispatch.test.ts
  ```
- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```console
  $ node ./node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/dispatch.test.ts
  ✓ dispatch > ... (existing dispatch tests pass)
  ```
  The fence key now includes the inbound MessageSid. Two concurrent dispatches from different inbound messages produce different fence keys, so neither delivery is suppressed by the other. When MessageSid is unavailable (e.g., cron/system events), the fence falls back to per-target scoping.
- **Observed result after fix**: Replies to distinct inbound messages in the same group chat are no longer suppressed by each other. Each inbound message gets its own independent fence.
- **What was not tested**: Real multi-user WhatsApp group with concurrent @mentions (requires live WhatsApp deployment); automated regression for group-chat fence behavior

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: fix: surface safe terminal tool fallbacks
target.body:
## Summary

- Add opt-in terminal fallback metadata through agent-core, session tool definitions, subscribe handling, and the embedded runner.
- Surface safe terminal summaries for tool-loop and incomplete-turn exits, while keeping undeclared tool output neutral instead of leaking raw results.
- Declare structured fallbacks for `cron` and `web_fetch`, and keep Docker image builds from carrying stale `dist` output.
- Tighten delivery/side-effect evidence for channel replies, source-reply deliveries, context-engine pairing, compaction ownership, and heartbeat lifecycle handling.

## Contract note

- `terminalSummary` and `terminalResultFallback` are intentionally public tool metadata for safe terminal presentation.
- Tools opt in explicitly; undeclared raw tool output is not surfaced as a fallback.
- Generic tool activity, accepted child-session spawns, messaging sends, cron additions, and replay-invalid aborts continue to suppress fallback model replay so side effects are not duplicated.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.empty-error-retry.test.ts src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts src/agents/outcome-fallback-runtime-contract.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/result-fallback-classifier.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.does-not-append-text-end-content-is.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.suppresses-message-end-block-replies-message-tool.test.ts src/agents/agent-tool-definition-adapter.after-tool-call.test.ts src/agents/agent-tool-definition-adapter.after-tool-call.fires-once.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-owned-tool-runtime-contract.test.ts src/dockerfile.test.ts src/agents/terminal-reply.test.ts packages/agent-core/src/agent-loop.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`
- `pnpm lint --threads=8`
- `pnpm tsgo:prod`
- `pnpm check:test-types`
- `pnpm test:extensions:package-boundary`
- `pnpm build`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Channel-facing agents that ended a turn after successful tool work could reply with planning text, repeat promises, or expose unsafe raw fallback text instead of delivering the completed tool result.

Real environment tested: A live Discord-backed OpenClaw stack using the patched image and a connected bot. Identifying stack, bot, channel, image, and run IDs are intentionally omitted from this public PR.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Sent live prompts that exercised a scheduler/cron path and a `web_fetch` tool-loop timeout path through the running gateway.

Evidence after fix:

Redacted live channel output, scheduler path:

```text
The requested scheduler state cannot be checked from this runtime because no scheduler, cron, gateway, or exec tool is available in this channel session. I cannot claim a live cron check without that tool.
```

Redacted live channel output, `web_fetch` terminal fallback path:

```text
URL: https://example.com
Final URL: https://example.com
Status: 200
Title: Example Domain
Cached: true
Truncated: false
```

Observed result after fix: The channel received user-visible results for both proof cases. The `web_fetch` fallback included URL, final URL, status, title, cache state, and truncation state, with no raw unsafe tool dump. The scheduler path returned a direct capability blocker instead of a promise to perform an unavailable check.

What was not tested: Broad cross-channel E2E beyond Discord was not rerun. One unrelated live runtime metadata drift was observed during proof and is omitted here because it is not part of this patch.

target.title: feat(web-search): add SerpApi plugin with web search provider and 25+ specialized tools
target.body:
Add SerpApi plugin with web search provider and 31 specialized tools

## Summary

- OpenClaw had no SerpApi integration — agents could not use SerpApi's 100+ search engines or its specialized search verticals (flights, hotels, shopping, maps, YouTube, scholar, finance, news, etc.).
- Adds a new `serpapi` plugin that registers as a `web_search` provider (display name: **SerpApi Search**) and exposes 31 dedicated tools covering Google, Bing, DuckDuckGo, Yahoo, YouTube, Maps, Scholar, Finance, Flights, Hotels, Shopping, News, Events, Jobs, Amazon, eBay, Walmart, Tripadvisor, Trends, Autocomplete, Lens, AI Overview, Facebook/Instagram profiles, and Weather.
- Plugin id: `serpapi`; config: `plugins.entries.serpapi.*`; API key: `webSearch.apiKey` config or `SERPAPI_API_KEY` env var.
- Cache, timeout, language, and result count are configurable via plugin config.
- No changes to core or other plugins; uses existing `provider-web-search` SDK helpers.

### Tools

| Tool | Description |
|---|---|
| `serpapi_ai_overview` | Fetch the full Google AI Overview for a search using a page_token |
| `serpapi_amazon` | Search Amazon for products — titles, prices, ratings, Prime status, ASIN |
| `serpapi_amazon_product` | Fetch detailed Amazon product info by ASIN |
| `serpapi_autocomplete` | Google search autocomplete suggestions for a partial query |
| `serpapi_bing` | Web search via Bing — titles, URLs, snippets |
| `serpapi_duckduckgo` | Web search via DuckDuckGo — organic results, knowledge graph, news |
| `serpapi_ebay` | Search eBay listings — titles, prices, conditions, links |
| `serpapi_ebay_product` | Detailed eBay product info by product ID |
| `serpapi_events` | Google Events search — title, date, address, ticket links |
| `serpapi_facebook_profile` | Fetch a public Facebook profile |
| `serpapi_finance` | Stock prices, currency rates, and crypto via Google Finance |
| `serpapi_flights` | Real flight prices and itineraries via Google Flights |
| `serpapi_hotels` | Hotels and accommodation via Google Hotels — price, rating, amenities |
| `serpapi_immersive_product` | Detailed Google product info by page_token from Shopping results |
| `serpapi_instagram_profile` | Fetch a public Instagram profile |
| `serpapi_jobs` | Google Jobs search — titles, companies, locations, salary |
| `serpapi_lens` | Google Lens reverse image search |
| `serpapi_maps` | Local businesses, restaurants, POIs via Google Maps |
| `serpapi_maps_reviews` | Reviews for a place on Google Maps |
| `serpapi_news` | Recent articles via Google News — headlines, sources, dates, URLs |
| `serpapi_scholar` | Academic papers, research articles, and citations via Google Scholar |
| `serpapi_shopping` | Google Shopping — product names, prices, stores, ratings |
| `serpapi_tripadvisor` | Destinations, hotels, restaurants, and attractions via Tripadvisor |
| `serpapi_trends` | Google Trends — interest over time, by region, related topics/queries |
| `serpapi_walmart` | Walmart product listings — titles, prices, ratings, links |
| `serpapi_walmart_product` | Detailed Walmart product info by product ID |
| `serpapi_weather` | Current weather or forecast for a location via Google |
| `serpapi_yahoo` | Web search via Yahoo! — titles, URLs, snippets |
| `serpapi_youtube` | YouTube search — videos, channels, shorts with metadata |
| `serpapi_youtube_transcript` | Fetch the transcript of a YouTube video |
| `serpapi_youtube_video` | YouTube video metadata — title, description, channel, views, chapters |

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: no SerpApi integration existed; this PR adds the full plugin.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on macOS.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm test extensions/serpapi`
- Evidence after fix:
  ```
   ✓  extensions/serpapi/src/serpapi-client.test.ts (11 tests) 53ms
   ✓  extensions/serpapi/src/serpapi-search-provider.test.ts (7 tests) 248ms

   Test Files  2 passed (2)
        Tests  18 passed (18)
     Duration  590ms
  ```
- Observed result after fix: all 18 tests pass across the client and web search provider.
- What was not tested: full cross-platform validation, automated CI live API calls.
- Proof limitations: automated CI live API calls not run; live behavior validated manually via Docker with a real SerpApi key.

## Tests and validation

- Added `serpapi-client.test.ts` — HTTP client, error handling, cache behavior, missing-key error.
- Added `serpapi-search-provider.test.ts` — provider registration, credential path, and config application.
- New plugin addition; no prior behavior to regress against.

## Risk checklist

- [x] User-visible behavior changed — new plugin, tools, and web search provider available when enabled.
- [x] Config/environment changed — new `plugins.entries.serpapi.*`; new `SERPAPI_API_KEY` env var.
- [x] Security/auth/network changed — new outbound HTTPS to `serpapi.com`, gated behind API key; key never logged.
- [ ] Breaking change to existing behavior — no existing behavior affected.

target.title: fix(fireworks): resolve catalog model params from plugin.json via core
target.body:
**TL;DR** the two bundled Fireworks Kimi models silently ran with the wrong limits — a not-yet-configured `kimi-k2p6` resolved with the default template's 256,000 context window instead of its own 262,144, and `kimi-k2p5-turbo` could lose its `unsupportedToolSchemaKeywords` compat. The plugin was re-implementing catalog lookup in TypeScript and preempting core's canonical `openclaw.plugin.json` resolution. This PR deletes the parallel path and defers manifest ids to core. ## Summary **What does this fix?** `resolveFireworksDynamicModel` cloned the *default* template for any id not already in user config — flattening per-model `contextWindow`/`maxTokens`/`compat` declared in the manifest. Not a crash; a silently-wrong value that affects context budgeting and tool-schema compat. **What changed?** - The dynamic hook now returns `undefined` for manifest-declared ids, so core's bundled static catalog (`resolveBundledStaticCatalogModel`) resolves them from `openclaw.plugin.json` — one canonical, JSON-driven path. The hook only synthesizes genuinely-dynamic ids (GLM text-only narrowing, unknown Kimi ids). - The manifest gains explicit `reasoning: false` on the two Kimi rows, matching their shipped off-only thinking profile (see risk section). - Removed the dead reasoning-strip in the Kimi stream wrapper: with catalog `reasoning:false` + the off-only profile, no `reasoning`/`reasoning_effort` field is ever built, so the `delete payloadObj.reasoning*` lines were no-ops. The load-bearing `thinking:{type:"disabled"}` toggle stays (Fireworks Kimi leaks chain-of-thought into visible content without it). - `isFireworksGlmModelId` moved to `model-id.ts`; catalog clones via `structuredClone` (deep, covers `compat`). **Out of scope:** no other providers, no core resolution changes, no gateway protocol, no new config/env surface. ## Linked context - **#92217 (same author) is the complementary companion**: it adds four Fireworks reasoning models (DeepSeek V4 Pro, MiniMax M2.7, GLM-5.1, GPT-OSS 120B) with `reasoning_effort` compat and per-family thinking profiles. The two PRs reinforce each other: this PR's manifest-id deferral makes the new rows' limits authoritative through core, and its early-return keeps #92217's dynamic-family compat patch serving only genuinely non-cataloged ids. They conflict textually in 3 files (`index.ts`/`model-id.ts`/`provider-catalog.ts`) where both made near-identical changes (same GLM-helper move, same `structuredClone` direction); whichever lands second rebases mechanically. - Core fallback path: `resolveModelWithRegistry` → plugin `resolveDynamicModel` → `resolveStaticCatalogFallbackModel` (`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.ts`), gated by `allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback`. ## Real behavior proof Behavior or issue addressed: bundled Fireworks Kimi models apply their per-model `contextWindow`/`maxTokens`/`compat` from `openclaw.plugin.json` instead of inheriting default-template limits; Kimi stream wrapper keeps chain-of-thought out of visible content. Real environment tested: VPS OpenClaw checkout on Oracle Linux (`aarch64`), branch `fix/fireworks-plugin-config`, real Fireworks API key. Exact steps or command run after this patch: `openclaw capability model inspect --model fireworks/accounts/fireworks/models/kimi-k2p6 --json` (and the `kimi-k2p5-turbo` equivalent); then live runs: `openclaw capability model run --model fireworks/accounts/fireworks/models/kimi-k2p6 --prompt "Reply with exactly: ok" --thinking off --json` and the same with `--thinking high`. Evidence after fix: terminal capture and screenshot below. `openclaw capability model inspect` shows `kimi-k2p6` with `contextWindow: 262144, reasoning: false` and `kimi-k2p5-turbo` with `contextWindow: 256000` plus `compat.unsupportedToolSchemaKeywords: ["not"]`; both live `openclaw capability model run` calls (thinking off and high) returned this copied live output with no `…` leak: ```json { "ok": true, "capability": "model.run", "transport": "local", "provider": "fireworks", "model": "accounts/fireworks/models/kimi-k2p6", "outputs": [{ "text": "ok", "mediaUrl": null }] } ``` Observed result after fix: each model surfaces its own distinct limits — the default template no longer flattens catalog metadata. On `main`, a not-yet-configured `kimi-k2p6` resolved with 256,000 instead of 262,144. What was not tested: nothing for the changed surface — catalog inspect and live runs (thinking off/high) were both exercised with a real key. Fresh-state embedded-runner resolution was additionally probed (see risk section). ## Tests and validation - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.test.ts`: 109 passed, incl. two new resolver-level regressions — a deferred Fireworks manifest id resolves from core's bundled static catalog, and user `openclaw.json` config for the same id still wins (custom `contextWindow` overrides; bundled catalog never consulted). Locks the precedence chain used by every neighbor provider. - `extensions/fireworks` vitest: 13 passed — new "defers manifest catalog models to core static-catalog resolution" test; per-model limits locked from the manifest; off-only thinking-profile coverage retained; the test that only covered the deleted reasoning-strip was removed with it. - `check:test-types`, `tsgo:extensions(+test)`, oxlint, oxfmt: clean. - Autoreview (claude sonnet-4-6, medium): "patch is correct (0.93), no accepted/actionable findings". ## Risk checklist - **ClawSweeper [P2] — resolution routing changes for two shipped ids** (plugin dynamic-template clone → core static-catalog fallback). Addressed three ways: (1) onboarded/configured installs resolve these ids from the model registry (generated from the same manifest) and never reach either fallback; (2) the entry points that can see a bare manifest id without registry coverage enable `allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback` (`runtime-llm.runtime.ts`, media-understanding, image tool, capability CLI with explicit override); (3) probed empirically on this head: a fresh state dir (no models.json, only the env API key) plus an embedded `openclaw agent --local` turn on deferred `kimi-k2p6` — the path where `allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback` is conditional — resolves and answers (`READY`). The alternative "better-code" shape — having the plugin resolve manifest rows itself — would keep the duplicate resolution path this PR exists to delete; deferral to core is the owner-boundary fix. - **ClawSweeper [P1] — explicit `reasoning: false` changes static-catalog metadata.** No consumer regression: the dynamic path already shipped `reasoning: false` for Kimi ids (`reasoning: !isKimiModel`), and the plugin's id-based off-only thinking profile applied regardless. The manifest change aligns static metadata with already-shipped runtime values — making implicit policy explicit *is* the better code here. - User config precedence unchanged and now regression-tested; no migration, no new config keys. - Net non-test LOC down (Source +1 overall per ClawSweeper; the rest is tests). ## Current review state What is the next action? Maintainer review — specifically the bot's "accept the static-catalog handoff" decision (its recommended option 1). A `@clawsweeper re-review` is warranted: the posted review was against `1794efbba18d`, superseded by current head `f24d47d708` (rebased onto `main` `301213a05f`; one trivial conflict in `model-id.ts` — main's new header comment vs the GLM helper move — resolved by keeping both). What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? Maintainer: provider/runtime-owner acceptance of the resolution-order handoff + ordering vs #92217 (whichever lands second rebases 3 files mechanically). Author/CI/external: nothing outstanding. Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? target.title: fix(reply): suppress direct tool-error progress leaks
target.body:
## Summary
- suppress text-only tool error progress in direct chats when the turn still produces a normal final reply
- keep the existing terminal fallback path intact so real failures still surface when there is no usable final answer
- add a regression test covering the reproduced Telegram DM cold-start leak

## Repro / proof
Concrete live repro from Joseph's Telegram DM on **June 3, 2026** after the machine had been off for about a week:

1. Power the system back on.
2. Start a direct Telegram conversation with a lightweight greeting like `Yo`.
3. The agent startup sequence tries to read expected daily memory files for today/yesterday.
4. If one of those files does not exist, the internal tool failure is emitted into the DM before or alongside the real reply.

Observed visible output:
- user: `Yo`
- assistant final reply: `Yo.`
- leaked tool-progress error: `⚠️ 🛠️ print lines 1-220 from memory/2026-06-03.md (agent) failed`

That is the wrong surface behavior for a trivial greeting. The user should get the greeting, and any internal failed read should stay internal unless the turn actually ends in failure.

## Root cause
`dispatch-from-config` was forwarding text-only `onToolResult(... isError: true ...)` payloads directly to user-visible delivery in direct chats. That bypassed the later terminal-warning policy, which already tries to avoid showing warning text when a normal reply exists.

## Fix
This change suppresses direct-chat text-only tool error progress payloads before delivery, while preserving the existing end-of-turn fallback logic. The result is:
- normal direct replies stay clean
- tool failures can still surface at the end of a truly failed turn
- media/approval payloads are unaffected
- verbose `full` mode behavior is unchanged

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Direct Telegram chats could leak text-only internal tool-error progress, such as a missing startup memory-file read, before/alongside a normal final reply. This patch keeps the normal final reply visible while suppressing that raw internal progress line.
- **Real environment tested**: Exact PR head `1579b5197f3938f6ec904f3e319c7514e7f88291` checked out from `jewseppi/openclaw` in `/tmp/openclaw-pr89975-proof.6sEupH/repo` on Joseph's OpenClaw host, using `pnpm 11.2.2` and the repository lockfile. A live Telegram Bot API dispatcher was wired into that exact-head `dispatchReplyFromConfig` path and sent to Joseph's real Telegram DM (`chat_id=6300969793`). The live Telegram direct conversation also reproduced the same missing-memory-file internal failure mode during this proof attempt without surfacing the raw `sed: can't read memory/2026-06-06.md` / `memory/2026-06-05.md` tool output as Telegram chat text.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
git clone --no-tags --depth 1 --branch fix/direct-tool-error-progress-leak-main https://github.com/jewseppi/openclaw.git /tmp/openclaw-pr89975-proof.6sEupH/repo
git -C /tmp/openclaw-pr89975-proof.6sEupH/repo rev-parse HEAD
pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
pnpm test -- --run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts
gh workflow run mantis-telegram-desktop-proof.yml --repo openclaw/openclaw -f pr_number=89975 -f instructions='telegram desktop proof: verify latest head suppresses the extra direct-chat tool-error warning while preserving the final reply.'
OPENCLAW_PROOF_HEAD=1579b5197f3938f6ec904f3e319c7514e7f88291 pnpm exec tsx /tmp/openclaw-pr89975-proof.6sEupH/live-telegram-dispatch-proof.ts
```

- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal capture from the exact-head checkout and live Telegram dispatch probe:

```text
$ git -C /tmp/openclaw-pr89975-proof.6sEupH/repo rev-parse HEAD
1579b5197f3938f6ec904f3e319c7514e7f88291

$ pnpm test -- --run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  37 passed (37)

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply.config.ts

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  191 passed (191)

[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 39.47s

$ gh workflow run mantis-telegram-desktop-proof.yml --repo openclaw/openclaw -f pr_number=89975 -f instructions='telegram desktop proof: verify latest head suppresses the extra direct-chat tool-error warning while preserving the final reply.'
could not create workflow dispatch event: HTTP 403: Must have admin rights to Repository.

$ OPENCLAW_PROOF_HEAD=1579b5197f3938f6ec904f3e319c7514e7f88291 pnpm exec tsx /tmp/openclaw-pr89975-proof.6sEupH/live-telegram-dispatch-proof.ts
[diagnostic] message dispatch completed: channel=telegram sessionId=unknown sessionKey=agent:main:telegram:direct:PR89975-LIVE-PROOF-2026-06-07T02-41-20-545Z source=replyResolver outcome=completed duration=577ms
[diagnostic] message processed: channel=telegram chatId=telegram:6300969793 messageId=unknown sessionId=unknown sessionKey=agent:main:telegram:direct:PR89975-LIVE-PROOF-2026-06-07T02-41-20-545Z outcome=completed duration=4462ms
{
  "marker": "PR89975-LIVE-PROOF-2026-06-07T02-41-20-545Z",
  "head": "1579b5197f3938f6ec904f3e319c7514e7f88291",
  "failedOutputText": "⚠️ 🛠️ PR89975-LIVE-PROOF-2026-06-07T02-41-20-545Z print lines 1-220 from memory/2026-06-03.md (agent) failed",
  "finalText": "PR89975-LIVE-PROOF-2026-06-07T02-41-20-545Z final reply: Yo.",
  "dispatchResult": {
    "queuedFinal": true,
    "counts": {
      "tool": 0,
      "block": 0,
      "final": 0
    }
  },
  "sends": [
    {
      "kind": "final",
      "text": "PR89975-LIVE-PROOF-2026-06-07T02-41-20-545Z final reply: Yo.",
      "telegramMessageId": 4201
    }
  ],
  "toolMessagesSent": 0,
  "finalMessagesSent": 1
}
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The exact-head live Telegram dispatcher attempted the failed progress payload `⚠️ 🛠️ PR89975-LIVE-PROOF-2026-06-07T02-41-20-545Z print lines 1-220 from memory/2026-06-03.md (agent) failed`, but sent only the final Telegram message `PR89975-LIVE-PROOF-2026-06-07T02-41-20-545Z final reply: Yo.` as Telegram message id `4201`. The exact-head regression coverage also proves the direct-chat dispatch path drops the text-only `isError` tool-progress payload and still sends the normal final reply (`Yo.`), while the embedded-runner coverage preserves real terminal warning fallback behavior for unacknowledged/unsafe failures.
- **What was not tested**: I could not produce maintainer-owned native Telegram Desktop Mantis video from this account. `workflow_dispatch` for `mantis-telegram-desktop-proof.yml` is repository-admin-only, and previous comment-trigger attempts were skipped/unauthorized because `jewseppi` only has read-level access on `openclaw/openclaw`.

## Test
- `pnpm test -- --run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`
- `pnpm test -- --run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`

## Related evidence
This came up during a broader cold-start recovery path after the machine had been powered off for days. There was also a separate stale-`dist` cold-start failure reported earlier in the week. That looks like a different root cause; this PR only fixes the leaked internal tool-error symptom above.

target.title: fix(tts): defer text settlement for final-mode TTS to eliminate churn (#83511)
target.body:
## Summary

When `messages.tts.mode = "final"`, the Telegram bot exhibited text "churn" — users would briefly see a text message appear, then it would be deleted and replaced by a voice note with the same text as caption. This happened on **both** the ACP dispatch path and the non-ACP dispatch path (`dispatch-from-config.ts`).

This PR eliminates the churn on **both paths** by introducing a `captionedFinalText` channel capability, suppressing live block delivery and partial streaming when captioned final TTS will be used, and correctly handling tagged auto-TTS mode with a split eligibility boolean.

## Root cause

```
Before (main):
  1. Block text / partial streaming delivers text to user immediately
  2. TTS synthesis runs (2-5 seconds)
  3. Voice note + caption delivered as separate message
  Result: user sees text first, then voice note = churn (#83511)

After (this PR):
  1. willUseCaptionedFinalTts = true (channel supports captioned voice + final TTS mode)
  2. Partial streaming suppressed, block text delivery suppressed
  3. TTS synthesis runs, text accumulated silently
  4. Voice note + caption delivered as single message (or text fallback if TTS fails)
  Result: user sees only the voice note with caption = no churn
```

### Tagged auto-TTS mode

When `messages.tts.auto: "tagged"`, TTS only fires for replies containing `[[tts:...]]` directives. Three additional issues were discovered and fixed:

1. **Split boolean**: `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` was doing two jobs — gating live text suppression AND caption eligibility. Split into `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` (live text suppression, excludes tagged) and `canCaptionFinalTts` (caption eligibility, includes tagged).

2. **Block TTS gate**: The accumulated block TTS fallback was gated on `replies.length === 0`, but Anthropic streaming returns both block callbacks and a final reply payload. Changed to gate on `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` and `queuedFinal` instead.

3. **Partial streaming leak**: `onPartialReply` was active for tagged mode, sending raw `[[tts:text]]...[[/tts:text]]` directive text to Telegram during streaming, creating a duplicate text message before the voice+caption arrived. Fixed by gating `onPartialReply` with `canCaptionFinalTts` (covers both always and tagged modes) instead of `willUseCaptionedFinalTts`.

### Media block preservation

When captioned final TTS is active, block suppression is narrowed to **text-only** payloads. Blocks containing media (`mediaUrl`, `mediaUrls`), `presentation`, `interactive`, or `channelData` are delivered immediately with their text stripped (to avoid duplicating the caption). This applies to both ACP and non-ACP dispatch paths.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts` | `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` + `canCaptionFinalTts`: suppress partial streaming and block text for both always and tagged modes; narrow block suppression to text-only payloads (media blocks delivered with text stripped); `sendFinalPayload` returns `deliveredMedia`; block TTS gate changed from `replies.length === 0` to `!deliveredFinalTtsMedia && !queuedFinal` |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` | Regression tests: tagged directive-only voice+caption, tagged+always both-block-and-final delivery, partial suppression for tagged TTS on caption-capable channels, plain tagged block delivery, media block delivery with text stripped, text-only block suppression |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts` | `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` gate: suppress ACP block delivery and defer text settlement; `canCaptionFinalTts` for tagged mode; `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` tracking |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.ts` | `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` state tracking + `hasDeliveredFinalTtsMedia()` accessor; narrow `suppressBlockUserDelivery` to text-only blocks (media blocks delivered with text stripped) |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts` | Regression tests: media block delivery with text stripped under `suppressBlockUserDelivery`, text-only block suppression |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts` | Regression tests: deferred settlement, block suppression, tagged streaming, tagged directive TTS, ACP live text deferral |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.ts` | `deferLiveTextUntilFinalFlush` option to suppress live text flushing for tagged TTS |
| `src/tts/directives.ts` | `resolveDirectiveOnlyTtsCaptionText` parser helper to extract clean caption from directive-only responses |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.ts` | `captionedFinalText: true` capability declaration |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts` | Test for capability declaration |
| `src/channels/plugins/types.core.ts` | JSDoc for `captionedFinalText` capability contract |
| `src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts` | Capability seam test |
| `docs/tools/tts.md` | Document `captionedFinalText` capability: opt-in criteria via `capabilities.tts.voice`, tagged mode behavior, text-only fallback, plugin API snippet |
| `docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md` | Document `captionedFinalText` capability in channel plugin SDK guide: opt-in declaration, media block preservation, fallback behavior, tagged mode |
| `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256` | Updated SDK API baseline hash |

## Tests

```
$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts
 ✓ 202 passed (202)

$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts
 ✓ 29 passed (29)

$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
 ✓ 50 passed (50)

$ npx vitest run extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts
 ✓ 11 passed (11)

$ npx vitest run src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts
 ✓ 1 passed (1)

Total: 293 tests passed across 5 test suites
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Telegram TTS final-mode text-before-voice churn on both ACP and non-ACP dispatch paths, plus tagged mode partial preview leaking raw `[[tts:text]]` directives, plus media block suppression during captioned final TTS.

- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at commit `05b4483a8f`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0. Live Telegram bot (`@thomas_fu_bot`) with Microsoft TTS (`zh-CN-XiaoxiaoNeural`), `messages.tts.mode: "final"`.

- **Failing proof before fix (live Telegram)**:

  ![Before fix — separate text message + voice message (churn)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-before-fix.png)

  User sent "hello" to the Telegram bot. The bot responded with **two separate messages**: a text message AND a voice note — instead of a single voice note with caption.

- **Exact steps after this patch**:
  1. Built and installed from current head: `npm run build && npm pack && npm install -g openclaw-2026.6.2.tgz`
  2. Registered and restarted Gateway: `openclaw gateway install && openclaw gateway restart`
  3. Tested with `messages.tts.auto: "always"` and `messages.tts.auto: "tagged"` — sent plain messages and directive-bearing messages to `@thomas_fu_bot` on Telegram

- **Evidence after fix — Always mode (`messages.tts.auto: "always"`) at commit `05b4483a8f`**:

  ![Always mode — voice with caption, single message](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-always-mode.png)

  Always-mode replies produce a single voice note with caption text. No text churn, no separate text bubble.

- **Evidence — Media block preservation with captioned final TTS at commit `05b4483a8f`**:

  ![Media block preserved — image delivered + voice note with caption](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-media-block.png)

  When the bot generates an image during a TTS-enabled conversation (`auto: "always"`), the image is delivered immediately while text is deferred to the voice note caption. No media blocks are suppressed, and caption text does not duplicate as a separate text message.

- **Observed result after fix**: Always TTS auto mode delivers voice notes as single messages with caption text on Telegram. Media blocks (images, files) are preserved and delivered immediately with text stripped. No text flash, no duplicate messages, no media loss.

- **What was not tested**: Other TTS providers (only Microsoft TTS tested live). Group chat TTS (tested in DM only). Other channels with `captionedFinalText` capability (only Telegram tested). Tagged mode live proof pending — covered by unit tests (partial suppression + directive-only caption regression tests added).

Closes #83511
