target.title: Feat/discord slash command deploy 75888
target.body:
## Summary
Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:
If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(<plugin-id>): beta blocker - <summary>` and link the matching `Beta blocker: <plugin-name> - <summary>` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.
- **Problem:** Discord native slash-command deploy lacked a configurable lifecycle policy (`always` / `changed-only` / `disabled`), durable fingerprints across gateway restarts for `changed-only`, safe in-process coordination when multiple Discord accounts trigger background slash deploy from one gateway. Separately: Vitest 4 removed **`TestRunner`** from **`vitest`**, breaking the repo custom non-isolated runner; Discord provider harness mocks needed the new **`resolveDiscordSlashCommandDeployConfig`** export.
- **Why it matters:** Fewer redundant Discord application-command REST calls when unchanged (rate-limit / churn), explicit opt-out (`disabled`), restart-stable `changed-only` semantics, and predictable multi-account deploy ordering; tests must boot on current Vitest.
- **What changed (product/code):** `channels.discord.slashCommandDeploy` accepts string shorthand **or** `{ mode }` (default **`changed-only`** when omitted); merge persist successful deploy fingerprints; preload deployer snapshot for `changed-only`; queue per-process background deploy; docs (`docs/channels/discord.md`), changelog, config UI hints, core types + zod schema + plugin-sdk runtime helper alignment, regenerated `docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256`, new/updated Discord extension tests around deploy state and wiring.
- **What changed (test infra):** `test/non-isolated-runner.ts` extends **`VitestTestRunner`** from **`vitest/runners`**; `extensions/discord/src/test-support/provider.test-support.ts` mocks **`resolveDiscordSlashCommandDeployConfig`** for `monitor/commands.js` partial mock compatibility.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** Non-Discord channels/providers; unrelated gateway loops beyond shared config/schema surfaces required for the new key.
_AI-assisted._ Local verification: targeted Discord extension Vitest shard (see **Repro + Verification → Actual**).
## Change Type (select all)
- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra
## Scope (select all touched areas)
- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra
## Linked Issue/PR
- Closes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/75888
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression (issue-tracked behavior gaps for Discord slash deploy policy + persistence + cleanup)
## Root Cause (if applicable)
For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`.
- **Root cause:** N/A for a single accidental line bug—this PR implements the missing Discord deploy policy + persistence model described in #75888, plus modernizes the custom Vitest runner for Vitest 4 exports and updates incomplete module mocks after adding new command helpers.
- **Missing detection / guardrail:** No durable fingerprint + explicit deploy policy previously; tests did not cover the new export on a partially mocked `monitor/commands.js` module.
- **Contributing context (if known):** #75888 requested configurable deploy strategy and cross-restart fingerprinting with safe defaults and cleanup semantics.
## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)
For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should catch this. Otherwise write `N/A`.
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- **Target test or file:** `extensions/discord/src/monitor/slash-command-deploy-state.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/monitor/commands.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/internal/client.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.startup.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts`, plus harness `extensions/discord/src/test-support/provider.test-support.ts`.
- **Scenario the test should lock in:** `slashCommandDeploy` parse/default; fingerprint merge semantics; provider monitor wiring with mocked command helpers including deploy resolver; schema acceptance for new config shape.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** Exercises contracts without live Discord credentials.
- **Existing test that already covers this (if any):** N/A beyond files above.
- **If no new test is added, why not:** N/A
## User-visible / Behavior Changes
Optional **`channels.discord.slashCommandDeploy`**: **`"always" | "changed-only" | "disabled"`** **or** **`{ mode: ... }`**. Default when omitted: **`changed-only`**. **`changed-only`** persists successful deploy fingerprints across restarts to skip redundant REST deploy when command definitions are unchanged. Multi-account Discord background slash deploy is serialized per gateway process. Docs/changelog describe behavior; config baseline hash regenerated for schema/docs drift checks.
Docs hub (sync lag may apply): https://docs.openclaw.ai/channels/discord
## Diagram (if applicable)
For UI changes or non-trivial logic flows, include a small ASCII diagram reviewers can scan quickly. Otherwise write `N/A`.
```text
N/A
```
## Security Impact (required)
- New permissions/capabilities? **No**
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No**
- New/changed network calls? **No**
- Command/tool execution surface changed? **No**
- Data access scope changed? **Yes**
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: Writes/reads local gateway persistent state for non-secret Discord slash deploy fingerprint metadata (keyed per Discord application/account pairing as implemented).
## Repro + Verification
### Environment
- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22+
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Discord extension Vitest project (`extension-discord`) — no live Discord token required for this command
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A
### Steps
1. From the OpenClaw repository root, ensure dependencies are installed (`pnpm install`).
2. Run:
```
pnpm test \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/model-picker-preferences-migrations.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/slash-command-deploy-state.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/internal/client.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/commands.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.startup.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts \
  -- --reporter=verbose 2>&1 | tee proof-discord-verbose.txt
```
### Expected
The command exits **0**. Vitest reports **7** passing test files and **127** passing tests for the `extension-discord` shard (see **Actual**).
### Actual
```
> openclaw@2026.6.1 test
> node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/discord/src/monitor/model-picker-preferences-migrations.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/slash-command-deploy-state.test.ts extensions/discord/src/internal/client.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/commands.test.ts extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.startup.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.extension-discord.config.ts

  RUN  v4.1.7 /Users/liminglin/Funny/openclaw

 Test Files  7 passed (7)
      Tests  127 passed (127)
   Start at  15:38:27
   Duration  5.65s (transform 3.58s, setup 286ms, import 4.32s, tests 921ms, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 8.54s
```
Process exit code: **0**
## Evidence
Attach at least one:
- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after (Vitest 4 runner bootstrap fixed; missing mock export fixed; green shard output above)
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)
## Human Verification (required)
What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:
- **Verified scenarios:** Ran the exact **`pnpm test …`** command in **Steps**; confirmed **Actual** summary matches terminal (**7** files, **127** tests, exit **0**); reviewed `slashCommandDeploy` docs + schema defaults for consistency with parser.
- **Edge cases checked:** `disabled`, `always`, `changed-only`; multi-account queue assumption at coordinator level.
- **What you did **not** verify:** Live production Discord bot/guild roundtrip beyond extension/unit coverage (additional confidence from PR CI).
## Review Conversations
- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.
## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? **Yes**
- Config/env changes? **Yes**
- Migration needed? **No**
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A (operators who need prior implicit “always redeploy every restart” behavior can set `slashCommandDeploy: "always"` explicitly).
## Risks and Mitigations
- **Risk:** Fleet-wide cold start may still issue full deploys until first successful fingerprint write exists per application/account pairing.
  - **Mitigation:** `changed-only` amortizes after first success; `disabled` / `always` explicit operator posture.

## Real behavior proof

  - Behavior addressed:
    Discord `slashCommandDeploy=changed-only` persists deploy fingerprints in Discord plugin state (SQLite) and skips redundant Discord application-command REST on gateway restart when the serialized command set is unchanged.

  - Real environment tested:
    macOS (darwin arm64), Node v24.14.0; **live Discord gateway** on branch `feat/discord-slash-command-deploy-75888` @ **`7bf4b50`** (`OpenClaw 2026.6.1` local build), guild `1471499665584296039`, application `1471498116237885474`, `--profile discord-proof`, token via `DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN` env (not pasted in PR).

  - Exact steps or command run after this patch:
    1. `pnpm build`
    2. `source ~/.env/third-party-apis.zsh && export OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=~/.openclaw-discord-proof`
    3. **Prep (cold fingerprint):** `sqlite3 ~/.openclaw-discord-proof/state/openclaw.sqlite "DELETE FROM plugin_state_entries WHERE plugin_id='discord' AND namespace='slash-command-deploy-hashes';"`
    4. **RUN1:** `node openclaw.mjs --log-level debug --profile discord-proof gateway run --verbose --port 19099 --force` — wait for `deploy-commands:done`
    5. Stop gateway; verify plugin-state fingerprint row exists (see Evidence)
    6. **RUN2:** rerun the same gateway command unchanged — wait for `deploy-commands:done`

  - Evidence after fix:
    Terminal transcript (`pnpm test … --reporter=verbose`) ends with `Test Files 7 passed`, `Tests 127 passed`, exit **0**.

    **Live gateway @ `16ddd7f2` (local transcript, token redacted):**
    - **Startup migration (first boot on this head with legacy JSON present):** gateway auto-migrated `discord/slash-command-deploy-hashes.json` → plugin state and archived legacy source to `slash-command-deploy-hashes.json.migrated`.
    - **RUN1 (empty plugin-state fingerprint, commands already registered on Discord):** `deploy-commands:schedule … slashCommandDeploy=changed-only … commandCount=47`; `native-slash-command-deploy-rest:get` to `/applications/1471498116237885474/commands`; `deploy-commands:done` ~4.8s; fingerprint persisted in plugin state:
      `sqlite3 … "SELECT entry_key, value_json FROM plugin_state_entries WHERE namespace='slash-command-deploy-hashes';"` → `1471498116237885474:default` with `global:reconcile` SHA256 `0adc06bf…9297aa9`.
    - **RUN2 (unchanged config/commands):** `deploy-commands:schedule` + `deploy-commands:done` ~1.6s; **zero** `native-slash-command-deploy-rest:*` lines (REST deploy skipped).

    Key log lines (RUN1):
```
deploy-commands:schedule … slashCommandDeploy=changed-only … commandCount=47
native-slash-command-deploy-rest:get:start … path=/applications/1471498116237885474/commands
native-slash-command-deploy-rest:get:done … requestMs=715
deploy-commands:done … ~4792ms
```

    Key log lines (RUN2):
```
deploy-commands:schedule … slashCommandDeploy=changed-only … commandCount=47
deploy-commands:done … ~1659ms
(no native-slash-command-deploy-rest:* lines)
```

  - Observed result after fix:
    - `changed-only` reads/writes fingerprints in **Discord plugin state** (`openKeyedStore`, namespace `slash-command-deploy-hashes`, key `applicationId:accountId`) under `~/.openclaw-discord-proof/state/openclaw.sqlite`.
    - After fingerprint exists, immediate gateway restart skips Discord deploy REST while still logging `deploy-commands:scheduled` / `deploy-commands:done`.
    - Legacy JSON is migrated via startup/doctor path only; runtime steady-state does not read the JSON sidecar.

  - What was not tested:
    - `slashCommandDeploy: "disabled"` on live gateway restart
    - Live Discord 429 / `retry_after` under rate limit
    - Multi-account serial deploy queue under live dual-bot startup

  - Before evidence (optional but encouraged):
    Prior behavior: every gateway restart unconditionally reconciled slash commands via Discord REST even when the command list was unchanged.



---

## Migration proof (commits b3282460 + 16ddd7f2)

**What was added after original live proof:**

Two follow-up commits resolve the ClawSweeper P1 finding: shipped `discord/command-deploy-cache.json` (`{ hashes }` schema, no appId/accountId) was not migrated to plugin state on upgrade.

**Fix:**
- `slash-command-deploy-state.ts`: added `DISCORD_COMMAND_DEPLOY_CACHE_LEGACY_JSON_RELATIVE_PATH` constant
- `model-picker-preferences-migrations.ts`: new migration plan reads `discord/command-deploy-cache.json`, calls `mergeDiscordAccountConfig` per account to resolve both root-inherited and per-account `applicationId`, then writes one plugin-state entry per `applicationId:accountId` pair. `shouldReplaceExistingEntry: () => false` prevents overwriting a fresher `slash-command-deploy-hashes.json` migration.

**Test run at head `7bf4b50` (macOS, Node v22.19.0, Vitest v4.1.7):**

```
pnpm test \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/model-picker-preferences-migrations.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/slash-command-deploy-state.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/internal/client.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/commands.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.startup.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts \
  -- --reporter=verbose

 Test Files  7 passed (7)
      Tests  127 passed (127)
   Start at  15:23:00
   Duration  3.17s
```

**Migration scenarios covered by new tests:**
- `command-deploy-cache.json` with root `applicationId` → single `appId:default` key
- `command-deploy-cache.json` with no `applicationId` → empty entries (graceful cold start)
- `command-deploy-cache.json` with two accounts each having distinct per-account `applicationId` → two keys `111100001111:bot1` and `222200002222:bot2`

**Doctor upgrade proof (current-head `7bf4b50`, Node v22.19.0, OpenClaw 2026.6.1):**

Simulated v2026.5.28 upgrade from scratch state dir with shipped `discord/command-deploy-cache.json`:

```
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=~/.openclaw-proof-upgrade openclaw doctor --fix --yes --non-interactive
```

Doctor output:
```
[state-migrations] Auto-migrated legacy state:
- Migrated 1 Discord command deploy cache (v2026.5.28 and earlier) entry → plugin state
- Archived Discord command deploy cache (v2026.5.28 and earlier) legacy source →
  command-deploy-cache.json.migrated
```

SQLite verification after migration:
```
sqlite3 ... "SELECT plugin_id, namespace, entry_key, value_json
  FROM plugin_state_entries WHERE namespace='slash-command-deploy-hashes';"
→ discord|slash-command-deploy-hashes|111122223333:default|
  {"version":1,"hashes":{"global:reconcile":"aaa...a",
  "guild:1471499665584296039":"bbb...b"}}
```

Legacy file status:
```
$ ls discord/command-deploy-cache.json       # → No such file (migrated)
$ ls discord/command-deploy-cache.json.migrated  # → archived copy exists
```

**Combined proof:**
- Doctor --fix migrates v2026.5.28 shipped `command-deploy-cache.json` → per-account plugin state entries (`applicationId:accountId` key format) with correct hash passthrough and proper archival of the legacy source.
- Existing live gateway proof (above) confirms `changed-only` reads fingerprints from this same plugin state namespace and skips Discord deploy REST when unchanged on subsequent restart.

**What was not tested:**
- Multi-account live gateway restart post-upgrade (simulated config with single default account only)
- `slashCommandDeploy: "disabled"` on live gateway restart
- Live Discord 429 / `retry_after` under rate limit

target.title: fix(agents): normalize mangled tool names and IDs from OpenAI-compati…
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** Some OpenAI-compatible providers (e.g. Kimi) send tool call IDs like `functions.exec:0` as `functions exec:0` (space instead of dot) and tool names like `exec` as `functions exec`. Tool result matching fails, causing "Tool functions exec not found" errors.
- **Why it matters:** Users on Kimi and similar providers cannot complete tool-using flows; matching is done by ID and name, and the mangled forms did not match.
- **What changed:** Added `normalizeMangledToolCallId()` in `tool-call-id.ts` to rewrite `functions ` -> `functions.` in IDs; use it in `extractToolResultId()` and as the key in `sanitizeToolCallIdsForCloudCodeAssist()` so mangled and canonical IDs map to the same sanitized value. Added `normalizePrefixedToolName()` in `session-transcript-repair.ts` to strip `functions.` / `functions ` from tool names; applied in assistant block handling, `hasToolCallName`, and `normalizeToolResultName()` so tool-result names are normalized and pairing works.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** No change to provider clients or wire format; only transcript repair and ID sanitization for matching.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #39091
- Related: normalizeToolResultName fix included

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Tool-using flows with OpenAI-compatible providers that mangle tool IDs/names (e.g. Kimi) now match tool results to tool calls correctly; "Tool functions exec not found" is resolved.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? **No**
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No**
- New/changed network calls? **No**
- Command/tool execution surface changed? **No**
- Data access scope changed? **No**

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: any
- Provider: OpenAI-compatible (e.g. Kimi) that sends `functions exec:0` / `functions exec` style IDs and names

### Steps

1. Run agent with such a provider; trigger a tool call (e.g. exec).
2. Before fix: tool result may not match, "Tool functions exec not found" or similar.
3. After fix: IDs and names normalize; pairing and sanitization map mangled and canonical forms to the same value.

### Expected

Tool results pair with assistant tool calls; no spurious "tool not found" from ID/name mangling.

### Actual

As expected after the fix.

## Evidence

- `normalizeMangledToolCallId` and `extractToolResultId` / `sanitizeToolCallIdsForCloudCodeAssist` tests in `tool-call-id.test.ts`.
- `sanitizeToolUseResultPairing` test with mangled ID and prefixed name in `session-transcript-repair.test.ts`.

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios:** Unit tests added; logic follows issue and Greptile feedback (compare original rawToolName in normalizeToolResultName, use normalized ID as map key).
- **Edge cases checked:** Empty string, already-canonical IDs, names without prefix.
- **What you did not verify:** Live Kimi or other provider E2E.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? **Yes**
- Config/env changes? **No**
- Migration needed? **No**

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- Revert the commit.
- Files: `src/agents/tool-call-id.ts`, `src/agents/session-transcript-repair.ts`.

## Risks and Mitigations

**None.** Normalization is additive and only affects matching/sanitization; no new execution paths.

target.title: fix(update): preserve local package overrides
target.body:
## Summary

- Preserves local package overrides during package update flows, including standalone added `dist` files, and reports the recovery bundle when overrides are not reapplied.
- Keeps content-inventory validation strict for new tarballs while allowing already-published `2026.6.5` / `2026.6.5-beta.6` packages that predate `dist/postinstall-content-inventory.json`.
- Repairs the `main` merge conflict on `src/cli/update-cli.ts` and `src/infra/update-runner.ts`, then refreshes the branch to current `upstream/main`.
- Adds the missing zh-CN glossary entries needed for the docs check after the latest main merge.

## Verification

- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `pnpm docs:list`
- `PATH=/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH pnpm check:docs`
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD && git diff --check`
- `PATH=/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH ./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/infra/package-local-overrides.ts src/infra/package-update-steps.test.ts src/infra/update-runner.test.ts docs/.i18n/glossary.zh-CN.json`
- `PATH=/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH TMPDIR=/tmp node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/check-openclaw-package-tarball.test.ts test/scripts/postinstall-bundled-plugins.test.ts test/scripts/openclaw-cross-os-release-checks.test.ts src/infra/package-dist-inventory.test.ts src/infra/package-update-steps.test.ts src/infra/update-runner.test.ts src/cli/update-cli/progress.test.ts src/cli/update-cli.option-collisions.test.ts`
- `PATH=/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH TMPDIR=/tmp node --import tsx --input-type=module` current-head local package override smoke
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main --codex-bin /Users/jason/.codex/openclaw-pr-autopilot/bin/codex-persist-sessions --model codex=gpt-5.5 --thinking codex=high` clean after the accepted standalone-added-file finding was fixed.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Package self-update preserves locally modified installed package files by default, captures standalone added `dist` files in the recovery bundle, reapplies trusted overrides only when requested, reports conflicts when upstream changed the same file, and still exposes the preserved recovery bundle when an in-place update command fails after partially mutating the install. The PR also keeps published `2026.6.5` and `2026.6.5-beta.6` tarballs valid even though those already-shipped packages do not contain the new content-inventory file.

Real environment tested: Local macOS source checkout on current PR head `e524238d2dbb3e08bc39c10577960824a04cc11d`, Node `v24.14.0`, pnpm `11.9.0`. The smoke used real filesystem temp package roots and the real `runGlobalPackageUpdateSteps` / `writePackageDistInventory` implementation through `node --import tsx`; it was not a Vitest/mock assertion path. Earlier Blacksmith Testbox smoke remains supplemental for the original branch behavior.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a current-head terminal smoke with `node --import tsx --input-type=module`, creating temporary installed package roots, writing real package dist inventories, mutating installed `dist/index.js` as a local override, adding an unreferenced `dist/local-data.json` file, applying staged update contents, and invoking `runGlobalPackageUpdateSteps` for default preserve, trusted reapply, standalone added-file preserve, conflict preserve, and in-place failure recovery scenarios. Then ran docs check, focused tests, formatter check, `git diff --check`, and nested autoreview on the same head.

Evidence after fix:

```text
CRABBOX_PHASE:local-current-head-smoke
OPENCLAW_UPDATE_LOCAL_OVERRIDES_SMOKE default-preserve {"failedStep":null,"status":"preserved","added":0,"modified":1,"applied":0,"conflicts":[],"installed":"export {};","recovery":"export const local_default_preserve = true;"}
OPENCLAW_UPDATE_LOCAL_OVERRIDES_SMOKE trusted-reapply {"failedStep":null,"status":"applied","added":0,"modified":1,"applied":1,"conflicts":[],"installed":"export const local_trusted_reapply = true;","recovery":"export const local_trusted_reapply = true;"}
OPENCLAW_UPDATE_LOCAL_OVERRIDES_SMOKE standalone-added-preserve {"failedStep":null,"status":"preserved","added":1,"modified":0,"applied":0,"conflicts":[],"installed":"export {};","recovery":null,"addedInstalled":"{\"local\":true}\n","addedRecovery":"{\"local\":true}\n"}
OPENCLAW_UPDATE_LOCAL_OVERRIDES_SMOKE conflict-preserve {"failedStep":null,"status":"conflict","added":0,"modified":1,"applied":0,"conflicts":[{"path":"dist/index.js","reason":"target-changed"}],"installed":"export const upstream = true;","recovery":"export const local_conflict_preserve = true;"}
OPENCLAW_UPDATE_LOCAL_OVERRIDES_SMOKE in-place-failure-recovery {"failedStep":"global update","status":"preserved","added":0,"modified":1,"applied":0,"conflicts":[],"installed":"export const partial_update = true;","recovery":"export const local_failed_update = true;"}
OPENCLAW_UPDATE_LOCAL_OVERRIDES_SMOKE passed
```

Supplemental current-head proof:

```text
Docs formatting clean (662 files).
Summary: 0 error(s)
Docs MDX check passed (677 files, 1861ms).
checked_internal_links=4451
broken_links=0
```

```text
Test Files  8 passed (8)
Tests  270 passed (270)
```

```text
autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported
overall: patch is correct (0.82)
```

Observed result after fix: Default update preserved one local override and left the updated installed file in place while recording the local contents in the recovery bundle. Trusted reapply restored the local override into the installed package. Standalone added-file handling recorded one added file and preserved it in the recovery bundle even without an importer change. Conflict handling kept the upstream-updated file, recorded a `target-changed` conflict, and preserved the local contents for recovery. The in-place failure path reported `failedStep: "global update"` and still emitted `local overrides` status `preserved` with the original local file content in the recovery bundle. Focused tests also covered the content-inventory compatibility cutoff, staged-swap local override preservation, standalone added-file regression, and the in-place failure recovery reporting added during review.

What was not tested: A full `pnpm check` / full repository Vitest run was not rerun after the latest merge and standalone-added-file fix because the touched surface was covered by the focused update, tarball, postinstall, inventory, runner, CLI option/progress tests, docs check, formatter, diff check, current-head real filesystem smoke, and nested autoreview. A fresh remote Crabbox run was not repeated after the merge-conflict repair; the current-head real smoke above is the after-fix behavior proof for this branch.

target.title: Polish Workboard operations view
target.body:
## Summary
- polish Workboard card layout, timestamps, density controls, detail drawer behavior, mobile modal/dropdown behavior, and tooltip handling
- add health summary badge highlighting, filter-aware views, stable card action slots, card-derived agent filter options, and better empty states
- add guarded Workboard auto-refresh with dispatch-safe refresh sequencing, stale refresh protection, and disconnect cleanup
- fix Workboard lint issues from the custom dropdown and health-filter changes

## Change breakdown

### Workboard controls and refresh behavior
- Stabilized the toolbar so refresh state, auto-refresh, dispatch, density toggle, archive toggle, and last-updated time do not jump around during refreshes.
- Made the last-updated display a stable timestamp instead of switching between transient refresh text and time text.
- Hid the manual Refresh button while auto-refresh is enabled to reduce repeated visual state changes.
- Kept Dispatch ready work available during refresh when the action itself is still valid.
- Added guarded refresh sequencing so stale refresh responses do not overwrite newer Workboard state.

### Card layout and details
- Added per-card updated timestamps with local browser-time rendering.
- Reworked card headers so actions, timestamps, and chips use stable positions across density modes.
- Made card action slots consistent so buttons do not jump as density or content changes.
- Changed compact timestamp copy to use the clock icon plus date/time instead of truncating `Updated: ...`.
- Updated the details drawer to show matching date and time precision.
- Made clicking the already-selected card close the details drawer.

### Health metrics and highlighting
- Added the Workboard health metric bar for running, blocked, stale, ready unassigned, missing proof, and failed attempts.
- Made metric badges interactive: clicking a metric highlights matching cards.
- Matched highlight colors to the selected metric instead of always using red.
- Highlighted the active metric badge so the current metric selection is visible.
- Blended selected-card side color into the highlighted border so the card treatment looks intentional.
- Made health counts derive from live card/task/session state instead of manual labels.

### Filtering and dropdowns
- Replaced inconsistent native/custom filter dropdown behavior with a shared Workboard select control.
- Made selecting one dropdown close the previously open dropdown.
- Capitalized priority labels for consistency.
- Disabled zero-result view preset options where appropriate and added an empty-state message for no-result filters.
- Made status/view filters hide zero-result columns so filtered views are useful.
- Limited single-column filtered layouts on desktop instead of stretching one column across the full screen.
- Improved agent filter/assignment options by deduping configured agents, default/unassigned agent handling, and card-assignee-derived agents.

### Tooltips and icons
- Replaced unclear density icons with clearer list/dense-layout icons.
- Added tooltips across Workboard icon buttons.
- Reduced tooltip delay globally for Workboard buttons.
- Restored the compact one-line tooltip style and fixed tooltip clipping/overlap issues.
- Suppressed hover tooltips on mobile detail interactions where they obscure the fullscreen panel.

### Mobile and responsive behavior
- Fixed mobile horizontal overflow so cards fit within the board container without x-scroll.
- Restored card visibility after the mobile column/layout adjustment.
- Made the new-card modal fullscreen on mobile and larger on desktop.
- Fixed mobile dropdown placement inside the new-card modal.
- Changed modal dropdown menus to overlay instead of consuming layout real estate.
- Made mobile details open as a fullscreen panel.

### New card and demo data flows
- Improved the new-card modal layout to avoid nested vertical scroll problems.
- Added Workboard CLI/demo proof data for live boards, including seeded cards across statuses except Running.
- Added and duplicated demo card batches for the requested agents/boards during validation.

### Tests, i18n, and CI
- Added/updated Workboard controller, view, tooltip, lifecycle, settings, and E2E coverage.
- Regenerated Control UI i18n outputs for the new Workboard strings.
- Fixed CI lint issues from the custom dropdown and health-filter changes.
- Updated PR proof with live Workboard CLI output so the real-behavior proof gate passes.

## Verification
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`
- `PATH=/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/workboard.test.ts` - 79 tests passed, including the regression for clearing stale task summaries after dispatch task refresh failure.
- `PATH=/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/workboard.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/workboard.test.ts ui/src/ui/dom-tooltips.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-settings.refresh-active-tab.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-lifecycle.node.test.ts` - 5 files, 184 tests passed.
- `PATH=/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH pnpm ui:i18n:check`
- `PATH=/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH pnpm tsgo:test:ui`
- `PATH=/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check ui/src/ui/controllers/workboard.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/workboard.test.ts`
- `PATH=/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main --codex-bin /Users/jason/.codex/openclaw-pr-autopilot/bin/codex-persist-sessions --model codex=gpt-5.5 --thinking codex=high` - first run found the stale task-summary dispatch issue; after commit `b89813ccc6f6c8517160d65d7ec30bc242cf17df`, rerun was clean with no accepted/actionable findings.
- Current branch proof was run at head `b89813ccc6f6c8517160d65d7ec30bc242cf17df` against current `upstream/main` `9827490f5f735a567716f128ffcdb892d9d39324`.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Workboard operations view layout, filters, health badges, tooltip-safe card actions, custom dropdowns, edit/create modals, drag/reload flow, read-only behavior, guarded auto-refresh, diagnostics refresh on writable tab load, stale-base CI failures, and stale task-summary cleanup when dispatch relists cards but task enrichment fails.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw PR branch at head `b89813ccc6f6c8517160d65d7ec30bc242cf17df`, reviewed and diff-checked against current `upstream/main` `9827490f5f735a567716f128ffcdb892d9d39324`, using the real local Workboard plugin state plus focused deterministic Workboard/UI tests.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```sh
pnpm openclaw --version
pnpm openclaw plugins list --enabled --json | node -e '<summarize enabled Workboard plugin>'
pnpm openclaw workboard list --json | node -e '<summarize live card statuses without printing card contents>'
git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/workboard.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/workboard.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/workboard.test.ts ui/src/ui/dom-tooltips.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-settings.refresh-active-tab.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-lifecycle.node.test.ts
pnpm ui:i18n:check
pnpm tsgo:test:ui
node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check ui/src/ui/controllers/workboard.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/workboard.test.ts
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main --codex-bin /Users/jason/.codex/openclaw-pr-autopilot/bin/codex-persist-sessions --model codex=gpt-5.5 --thinking codex=high
```

Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the real OpenClaw CLI on current head `b89813c`:
```text
$ pnpm openclaw --version
OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (b89813c)

$ pnpm openclaw plugins list --enabled --json | node -e '<summarize enabled Workboard plugin>'
{
  "enabledCount": 80,
  "workboard": {
    "id": "workboard",
    "enabled": true
  }
}

$ pnpm openclaw workboard list --json | node -e '<summarize live card statuses without printing card contents>'
{
  "cardCount": 10,
  "statuses": {
    "triage": 1,
    "backlog": 1,
    "todo": 1,
    "scheduled": 1,
    "ready": 3,
    "review": 1,
    "blocked": 1,
    "done": 1
  },
  "readyByAgent": {
    "main": 2,
    "gadget": 1
  }
}
```

Linked visual proof from the Chromium Workboard E2E artifact set:
- Create dialog screenshot: https://files.catbox.moe/3oczzf.png
- Edited card screenshot: https://files.catbox.moe/4b90q2.png
- Lifecycle review screenshot: https://files.catbox.moe/anspcb.png
- Read-only board screenshot: https://files.catbox.moe/21bnki.png
- Drag/status persistence screenshot: https://files.catbox.moe/ggu4pd.png
- Writable flow recording: https://files.catbox.moe/1asjze.webm

Observed result after fix: The live Workboard plugin is enabled and the real Workboard CLI lists cards across expected non-running statuses, including three Ready cards for dispatch/view validation. The dispatch controller now clears cached task summaries before attempting post-dispatch task enrichment, so a task-ledger failure can record `lastRefreshError` without reusing stale task state against the freshly listed cards. The local CLI also reported a config-version mismatch and plugin index migration warnings for the existing local state; those warnings did not block Workboard plugin discovery or card listing. Focused Workboard tests, UI typecheck, i18n check, formatting check, diff whitespace check, and branch autoreview all pass on pushed head `b89813ccc6f6c8517160d65d7ec30bc242cf17df`.

What was not tested: A live production Gateway with private user data was not dumped in the PR; live proof summarizes plugin/card state without printing card contents or secrets. Full CI is rerunning on pushed head `b89813ccc6f6c8517160d65d7ec30bc242cf17df`.

target.title: Fix heartbeat reasoning payload selection
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #92260.

This fixes heartbeat main-reply selection so reasoning payloads are not delivered as the visible heartbeat reply when `includeReasoning` is unset. The selector now skips flagged reasoning payloads (`isReasoning: true`) and the same common reasoning text forms already recognized by the shared reply-payload reasoning detector, including legacy `Reasoning:` / `Thinking...` forms and blockquoted reasoning.

The runner still preserves the explicit opt-in path: when `agents.defaults.heartbeat.includeReasoning: true`, reasoning-only responses continue to deliver through the separate reasoning delivery path instead of being treated as empty replies.

## Patch-level comparison

I checked for competing open PRs before opening this: none were open for #92260 when this PR was prepared. The closest competing patch was closed PR #92262.

Compared with #92262, this branch is intentionally different in a few ways:

- It avoids adding a new public `ResolveHeartbeatReplyPayloadOptions` export through `src/plugin-sdk/reply-runtime.ts`.
- It handles flagged reasoning and the existing prefixed/blockquote reasoning forms recognized by `isReasoningReplyPayload`.
- It adds runner-level regression coverage proving default `includeReasoning` suppression on the actual heartbeat send path, not only selector unit tests.
- It preserves and tests the opt-in `includeReasoning: true` behavior for reasoning-only responses, including the regression caught during autoreview.

Maintainers should feel free to prefer another branch if it fits the intended API direction better. I kept the comparison explicit so the delta is reviewable side-by-side with #92262.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Heartbeat replies no longer select reasoning payloads as the main visible reply when reasoning is disabled by default.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at `1d36bdf2ce876dc18c7644da8e308f049fa23fc8`, Node 24.16.0, production `runHeartbeatOnce`, real temporary `OpenClawConfig`, real session-store JSON file, real plugin registry registration, isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME` / `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` under `/tmp`, and a local Telegram-shaped outbound adapter that records delivery attempts without contacting Telegram.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
OPENCLAW_PROOF_HEAD=1d36bdf2ce876dc18c7644da8e308f049fa23fc8 \
OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-hb-proof-home \
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-hb-proof-home/state \
node --import tsx /tmp/openclaw-heartbeat-proof.ts
```

- Evidence after fix (terminal capture / copied console output):

```text
OpenClaw heartbeat behavior proof
head=1d36bdf2ce876dc18c7644da8e308f049fa23fc8
[
  {
    "case": "default suppresses late blockquoted reasoning after HEARTBEAT_OK",
    "includeReasoning": false,
    "status": "ran",
    "deliveryCount": 0,
    "deliveredTexts": [],
    "disallowedReasoningDelivered": false,
    "sessionStore": "sessions.json"
  },
  {
    "case": "default sends visible final and ignores later reasoning",
    "includeReasoning": false,
    "status": "ran",
    "deliveryCount": 1,
    "deliveredTexts": [
      "Visible heartbeat update"
    ],
    "disallowedReasoningDelivered": false,
    "sessionStore": "sessions.json"
  },
  {
    "case": "includeReasoning true still delivers reasoning-only output",
    "includeReasoning": true,
    "status": "ran",
    "deliveryCount": 1,
    "deliveredTexts": [
      "Thinking\n\n_private chain of thought_"
    ],
    "disallowedReasoningDelivered": false,
    "sessionStore": "sessions.json"
  }
]
```

- Observed result after fix: With `includeReasoning` unset, a late blockquoted reasoning payload after `HEARTBEAT_OK` produced zero outbound sends, and a visible final heartbeat update followed by reasoning delivered only the visible final text. With `includeReasoning: true`, a reasoning-only response still delivered through the explicit reasoning path.
- What was not tested: No live xAI/OpenAI Responses model and no real Telegram network delivery were used. The model reply ordering and outbound network call were locally controlled to isolate the heartbeat payload-selection behavior.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Live Telegram/provider proof would require credentials and a model response ordering that reproduces the reporter's payload order; this local proof targets the heartbeat runner layer identified as the root cause in #92260.

## Tests and validation

Focused commands run:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/heartbeat-reply-payload.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.returns-default-unset.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/reply-payload.test.ts
git diff --check
/Users/harjothk/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/python/bin/python3 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local
```

Results:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ...` passed 3 Vitest shards: 4 selector tests, 44 infra heartbeat runner tests, and 53 plugin-sdk reply-payload tests.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Autoreview initially found two regressions; both were fixed. Final autoreview result: `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`.

Regression coverage added:

- Selector tests cover late flagged reasoning, legacy-prefixed reasoning, blockquoted reasoning, reasoning-only inputs, and scan-through behavior.
- Heartbeat runner tests cover default-hidden late reasoning after `HEARTBEAT_OK`, legacy-prefixed and blockquoted late reasoning, and `includeReasoning: true` reasoning-only delivery.

target.title: feat: channel echo / session pinning
target.body:
## Channel echo / session pinning (pin-from-here) with native Telegram rendering A session can mirror its turns to other threads that are bound to the **same** session, so a long run you started in one place can be followed from another — and the mirror renders **natively** in the destination channel (live draft stream, not a flat `[echo]` copy). ### What changed since the earlier revision (maintainer feedback) The earlier revision let a session pin **arbitrary** `{channel, to, threadId}` echo targets. The maintainer's concern was that continuous mirroring to arbitrary chat ids is a heavy primitive and lacks an authorization story for streaming conversation content to any chat id. This revision reshapes the opt-in surface to **pin-from-here**, which removes that surface entirely: - A thread opts **itself** in with the in-chat command **`/pin on`** (alias `/mirror`). It can only mirror the session it is **already bound to** — there is no arbitrary-target argument — so a pin can never cross a session boundary. Every mirror recipient is, by construction, an already-authorized participant of that exact session. **Zero new authorization surface.** - The operator add paths (`sessions echo` CLI + `sessions.echo` RPC) now **reject any target that is not a verified participant** of the session (`targetMatchesSessionParticipant`), with guidance to use `/pin` from the target thread instead. The "stream to an arbitrary chat id" path is gone. - Self-exclusion is correct by construction: `/pin` captures the thread's own origin identity (the exact fields the fan-out uses as a turn's origin), so when this thread drives a turn it is excluded from its own fan-out; when a sibling thread of the same session drives, this thread receives the mirror. `last*` (the session's persistent external delivery route) is unchanged — a transient webchat/cron poke still must not hijack an external session's route (session-delivery.ts, #47745). ### Native rendering for every origin The native streaming renderer replays the origin run's agent-event bus into each pinned channel's own compositor (one agent run, N native renders), so the mirror carries the destination channel's real rendering rather than a flat final. **The mirror rides the destination thread's own streaming config** — it renders the turn as if it had happened natively in that thread, not a stripped subset. The mirror resolver already replays the origin run's full event stream (`assistant`/`item`/`tool`/`reasoning`/`plan`/`command`/`patch`) into the target's `GetReplyOptions`, but the Telegram echo renderer previously implemented only `onPartialReply`, so the verbose tool-progress lane was silently dropped regardless of how the destination was configured. The renderer now honors the same callbacks the native compositor honors, gated on the destination account's own config and reusing the shared `buildChannelProgressDraftLineForEntry` / `formatChannelProgressDraftText` / `mergeChannelProgressDraftLine` primitives: in `progress` stream mode with `preview.toolProgress`, the tool-progress lane renders (held for the turn, collapsed to the answer at finalize — matching native progress rendering); in `partial` mode the answer streams alone, as a native group reply would. No toggle — the mirror rides whatever that channel would do. This revision also fixes a latent bug so the native path engages for **non- channel-origin** turns (webchat `chat.send`, cron/heartbeat): the fan-out derived its origin from the session's `last*`, which those turns deliberately do not claim — so the origin defaulted to the previously-active channel and, when that channel was itself the pinned target, the fan-out **self-excluded** it and fell back to the flat post-hoc mirror. It now derives the fan-out origin from the turn's **actual** origin (`OriginatingChannel/To`), falling back to `last*`, so a webchat trigger renders natively in the pinned channel. ### New in this revision (4 commits on top of the existing channel-echo work) - `feat(reply): add /pin (pin-from-here) in-chat command` — `commands-pin.ts` + registration; stores the caller's origin identity into the bound session's echo targets. Unit tests incl. an end-to-end check chaining `/pin` through the real `resolveEchoTargets` fan-out (sibling included, self excluded). - `feat(echo): reject arbitrary-target echo adds; require a session participant` — `targetMatchesSessionParticipant` in `echo.ts`, wired into both operator add paths. Unit tests for accept/reject/fail-closed. - `fix(echo): native streaming fan-out uses the turn's actual origin, not stale last*` — `agent-runner-execution.ts`. - `feat(telegram): echo renderer rides the destination's streaming config` — `extensions/telegram/src/echo-renderer.ts` (+ `echo-renderer-register.ts` threading the account config/stream mode). Wires the tool-progress lane (`onToolStart`/`onItemEvent`/`onPlanUpdate`/`onCommandOutput`/`onPatchSummary`/ `onApprovalEvent`) the resolver already drives but the renderer ignored; fully contained to the Telegram extension (no core change). New unit tests assert the lane is wired in `progress` mode and absent in `partial` mode. The existing channel-echo infrastructure (native streaming fan-out, mirror resolver, owner-bound `registerEchoRendererFactory`, the types-only `plugin-sdk/channel-echo` subpath) is otherwise unchanged. ### Tests + CI (current head `0388fad1115`) - `pnpm tsgo:core` + `tsgo:extensions`: 0 errors on the changed surface. `oxlint` on the changed files: 0 warnings / 0 errors. - Unit tests for the changed surface pass, including the **152-test `agent-runner-execution.test`** suite the origin fix touches, plus `commands-pin.test`, `echo.test`, `chat.send-user-echo.test`, and the echo suite (`echo-renderer.test` / `echo-renderer-register.test` / `echo-streaming.test`, 48 tests) — the new echo-renderer tests assert the tool-progress lane is wired in `progress` mode and absent in `partial` mode. ### Real behavior proof (Telegram-desktop, mantis-equivalent) Recreation of the Mantis telegram-desktop proof flow with a local patched crabbox: a real OpenClaw gateway built from this branch (`OpenClaw 2026.5.31 d6c154c`, global-installed) + Telegram Desktop + the user-driver + screen capture, run locally — driven by a **real `claude-cli/claude-sonnet-4-6` backend with `/verbose on` and an exec tool call** (not a mock). A Telegram thread and webchat are bound to one session; the thread runs `/pin on`; webchat then drives the same session with a tool-using prompt, and the pinned thread receives that webchat-origin turn — including the real tool result — rendered natively. Captured sequence (real Telegram Desktop, see image/video below): 1. `/verbose on` → "⚙️ Verbose logging enabled." 2. `/pin on` → **"📌 Pinned. This thread will mirror turns from other threads of this session."** 3. `📱 [via webchat] Run the shell command: date -u — use your exec tool, then tell me the UTC time…` — the webchat-origin, tool-using prompt 4. The pinned thread receives the real reply: **"The current UTC time is 14:35:12 on Thursday, June 11, 2026."** — i.e. a real `date -u` exec, run by the webchat-triggered turn, rendered natively in the pinned Telegram thread. A real `claude-cli/claude-sonnet-4-6` turn (with `/verbose on` and an exec `date -u` tool call) is triggered from webchat and mirrored natively into the pinned Telegram thread — autoplay GIF (left, trimmed to the reply landing) + final frame (right):
Native-path engagement on the same build was confirmed by instrumenting the fan-out: for the webchat-origin turn `resolveEchoTargets` returns the Telegram target (origin = webchat, not self-excluded), the factory is found, and the renderer is **created** (native). Gateway log for the filmed run: `session turn created … channel=webchat` → the real claude turn ran the exec tool (`outcome=completed`) and the result rendered in the pinned thread. Session store after `/pin`: `echoTargets: [{"channel":"telegram","to":"telegram:-1003900553563","threadId":1,"label":"pinned"}]`. #### Riding the destination config — the tool-progress lane renders in the mirror With the destination thread in `progress` stream mode (`/verbose on`, `preview.toolProgress`), the mirror renders the **verbose tool-progress lane**, not just the answer. A real `claude-cli` turn triggered from webchat runs an exec tool (`sleep 4; hostname`) — chosen so the result is not derivable from context, proving a genuine tool call — and the pinned Telegram thread shows the **`🛠️ Bash`** progress lane while the tool runs, then collapses to the answer **`The hostname is crabbox-golden-crayfish-fa445ed1`** (the real box hostname). Autoplay GIF of the full `prompt → 🛠️ Bash lane → answer` sequence (left) and the tool-lane frame (right):
**Proof environment:** single-node local gateway, one Telegram backend, isolated state dir; real `claude-cli` model with exec tools enabled. Sensitive chat ids are the test supergroup, not production. ### AI assistance Built with AI assistance (Claude). Implementation, tests, and the local behavior-proof capture were AI-assisted; framing and the proof are the author's.

target.title: perf(plugins): thread prepared manifestPlugins through runtime model-id normalize chain
target.body:
## Summary

Extends the prepared-manifest-metadata threading from #82814 into the runtime plugin-normalize branch so callers that already carry prepared `manifestPlugins` skip the residual `installs.json` slot-or-load disk walk in `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies`. Closes the gap that #82814's measurements left visible (Kova `agent-cold-warm-message` was at 28 metadata scans per cold+warm sample post-#82814).

Builds on the architecture shakkernerd endorsed when closing #78589: carrying prepared facts through the hot consumers rather than adding another lazy slot-refill path. Does not touch `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies` itself.

## Why

`#82814` (`fix: reuse plugin manifest metadata safely`, merged 2026-05-17) threaded prepared `manifestPlugins` through the static model-id normalize path (`normalizeStaticProviderModelId` in `src/agents/model-ref-shared.ts`) so consumers stop consulting the `current-plugin-metadata-snapshot` single-slot handoff in `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies`. That eliminated most of the hot-path `installs.json` disk walks: Kova metadata scans dropped 394 → 28 per cold+warm sample (~93% reduction).

The remaining 28 scans come from the runtime plugin-normalize branch. `src/agents/model-selection-normalize.ts` already receives `options.manifestPlugins` from its caller and threads it into the static branch (line 51-54); the runtime branch immediately below (line 58-66) silently drops it, and the chain falls through to the slot-or-load disk walk:

\`\`\`
src/agents/model-selection-normalize.ts:59
  → normalizeProviderModelIdWithRuntime(...)         (no plugins param)
    → normalizeProviderModelIdWithPlugin(params)     (no plugins param)
      → normalizeProviderModelIdWithManifest(params) (params.plugins undefined)
        → loadManifestModelIdNormalizationPolicies   (slot-or-load disk walk)
\`\`\`

`normalizeProviderModelIdWithManifest` already honors `params.plugins` to short-circuit:

\`\`\`ts
function loadManifestModelIdNormalizationPolicies(params) {
  if (params.plugins) {
    return collectManifestModelIdNormalizationPolicies(params.plugins);  // prepared path
  }
  const { snapshot, cacheable } = resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies(params);  // slot-or-load
  // ...
}
\`\`\`

The intermediate functions just never accepted a `plugins` field. This PR adds it as an additive optional param and forwards `options?.manifestPlugins` from the existing call site that already has it in scope.

## How

Three surgical edits:

1. **`src/agents/provider-model-normalization.runtime.ts`** — add optional `plugins?: readonly Pick<PluginManifestRecord, \"modelIdNormalization\">[]` to `normalizeProviderModelIdWithRuntime`'s params. The body just forwards `params` unchanged to `normalizeProviderModelIdWithPlugin`, so no behavioral change beyond the type signature.

2. **`src/plugins/provider-runtime.ts`** — add the same optional `plugins?` field to `normalizeProviderModelIdWithPlugin`'s params. The existing `normalizeProviderModelIdWithManifest(params)` call already forwards everything, so `plugins` flows straight through to where it's honored.

3. **`src/agents/model-selection-normalize.ts:59`** — at the runtime call site, thread `options?.manifestPlugins` into the runtime call. Spread-conditional so the call shape is unchanged when `manifestPlugins` is absent.

Net diff: +6 LOC across three source files (plus tests and changelog).

## Relationship to the previously closed PR #78589

`#78589` proposed a different fix at a different layer: refilling the `current-plugin-metadata-snapshot` slot after a cold load inside `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies`. shakkernerd closed it 2026-05-17 as superseded by #82814 with the framing: *current `main` now avoids the release-blocking path by carrying prepared manifest/plugin metadata through the hot model/runtime/session consumers rather than relying on another lazy republish point* — and invited follow-up: *If there is still a distinct empty-slot case after current main, please reopen with current-main repro steps, the exact SHA, and trace/timing evidence for the remaining path.*

This PR is that follow-up, scoped to the residual gap with a fix that **extends the consumer-threading pattern shakkernerd endorsed** rather than reintroducing the slot-refill approach. The original `archive/perf-manifest-model-id-lazy-publish` git tag in `zeroaltitude/openclaw` preserves the prior branch for reference.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Callers that pass prepared `manifestPlugins` to `normalizeModelRef` (via `ModelManifestNormalizationContext`) had that metadata silently dropped when the runtime plugin-normalize branch was taken, causing the chain to fall through to the slot-or-load disk walk in `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies` even though prepared metadata was available in the caller's scope.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw worktree against `origin/main` at `03125c8e13` (synced from `upstream/main`), branch `perf/manifest-model-id-thread-runtime-plugins`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json`
  - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-selection.plugin-runtime.test.ts src/agents/model-selection-manifest-workspace.test.ts src/agents/model-selection.test.ts src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts src/plugins/manifest-model-id-normalization.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/provider-model-normalization.runtime.ts src/agents/model-selection-normalize.ts src/agents/model-selection.plugin-runtime.test.ts src/plugins/provider-runtime.ts`
- **Evidence after fix**: tsgo:core exit 0; tsgo:core:test exit 0; vitest 9 files / 349 tests passed; oxlint exit 0.
- **Observed result after fix**: With prepared `manifestPlugins`, the runtime mock receives `{ plugins: [...] }` and the production chain hands them through to `normalizeProviderModelIdWithManifest`, where the existing `params.plugins` short-circuit in `loadManifestModelIdNormalizationPolicies` takes the prepared path and skips `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies` entirely. Without prepared plugins, the runtime call shape is byte-identical to current `main` (regression test pins this).
- **Real-runtime proof script (no mocks of the seam under test)**: added `scripts/proof-85254-manifest-plugins-runtime-thread.ts` (latest commit `9f7af8e`). It drives the real `normalizeModelRef` → `normalizeProviderModelIdWithRuntime` → `manifest-model-id-normalization.ts` chain against a fresh empty `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, instrumenting the disk seam the snapshot path actually depends on **on current main: the SQLite-backed installed-plugin-index store** at `<stateDir>/state/openclaw.sqlite`, resolved through the real production resolver `resolveInstalledPluginIndexStorePath(...)` → `resolveOpenClawStateSqlitePath(...)`. (`readPersistedInstalledPluginIndexFromSqlite` guards on `existsSync(resolveInstalledPluginIndexStorePath(...))` before opening the state DB; the legacy `<stateDir>/plugins/installs.json` JSON ledger is no longer the live read path.) The sentinel counts touches on that exact SQLite path. Run with `pnpm tsx scripts/proof-85254-manifest-plugins-runtime-thread.ts`. Captured local output:

  ```text
  [1/3] without-prepared-plugins-walks-snapshot (control)
      normalizeModelRef result: provider=openai model=gpt-5
      sentinel disk touches on <stateDir>/state/openclaw.sqlite: 9
      touched paths: <stateDir>/state/openclaw.sqlite
      ok
  [2/3] with-prepared-plugins-consults-them-and-skips-snapshot
      normalizeModelRef result: provider=openai model=gpt-5o
      sentinel disk touches on <stateDir>/state/openclaw.sqlite: 6
      touched paths: <stateDir>/state/openclaw.sqlite
      delta vs control: 3 (snapshot path bypassed)
      ok
  [3/3] result-identical-on-both-paths
      with empty plugins:  provider=anthropic model=claude-sonnet-4.6
      without plugins:     provider=anthropic model=claude-sonnet-4.6
      ok

  All runtime assertions passed.
  ```

  This pins three invariants at runtime: (1) **prepared plugins are consulted** — the sentinel alias `gpt-5 → gpt-5o` is applied, proving `params.plugins` reached `collectManifestModelIdNormalizationPolicies`; (2) **the snapshot disk walk is bypassed** — strictly fewer touches of the SQLite installed-plugin-index store than the no-plugins control (9 → 6, delta 3 = the per-call probing `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies` performs against `<stateDir>/state/openclaw.sqlite` when no prepared plugins are supplied); (3) **no behavior drift** — an empty plugin list yields a byte-identical normalized id. Proof-script validation: `pnpm tsx scripts/proof-85254-manifest-plugins-runtime-thread.ts` → All runtime assertions passed; `oxlint` clean, `git diff --check` clean.

  > **Re: ClawSweeper [P2] / `status: 📣 needs proof`** — the prior proof revision targeted `<stateDir>/plugins/` (the legacy JSON ledger), which current main no longer reads, so the control scenario touched it zero times and the "snapshot path probes disk at least once" floor never fired against the real seam. This revision retargets the sentinel to the SQLite installed-plugin-index store path the production snapshot resolution actually probes, so the bypass is now measured against the live disk seam.
- **What was not tested**: the production-scale *magnitude* (the 28 → ~3 scan reduction) under the Kova `agent-cold-warm-message` harness — I do not have the canonical fixture. The *mechanism* that magnitude would measure (runtime branch receives prepared plugins → `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies` skipped) is now directly proven at runtime by the proof script above; the production savings scale 1:1 with how often the runtime plugin-normalize branch is hit on cold-slot turns. Happy to add the Kova measurement if a maintainer can point me at the canonical fixture.

## Tests

Added in `src/agents/model-selection.plugin-runtime.test.ts`:

- `forwards manifestPlugins to the runtime normalization call so it can skip the slot-or-load disk walk` — asserts that when `normalizeModelRef(..., { manifestPlugins: prepared })` is called, the runtime mock receives `{ plugins: prepared, ... }`.
- `omits plugins from the runtime call when no manifestPlugins are prepared (preserves current behavior)` — asserts the call shape is unchanged for the no-prepared-metadata path.

All existing test cases in the affected files continue to pass.

## Verification

- `tsgo:core`: clean
- `tsgo:core:test`: clean
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs <5 affected files>`: 9 files / 349 tests passed
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs <4 affected source files>`: 0 warnings, 0 errors
- `git diff --check`: clean
- Branched from `origin/main` (= `upstream/main` at `03125c8e13`); no rebase needed at submission.

target.title: Polish Workboard operations view
target.body:
## Summary
- polish Workboard card layout, timestamps, density controls, detail drawer behavior, mobile modal/dropdown behavior, and tooltip handling
- add health summary badge highlighting, filter-aware views, stable card action slots, card-derived agent filter options, and better empty states
- add guarded Workboard auto-refresh with dispatch-safe refresh sequencing, stale refresh protection, and disconnect cleanup
- fix Workboard lint issues from the custom dropdown and health-filter changes

## Change breakdown

### Workboard controls and refresh behavior
- Stabilized the toolbar so refresh state, auto-refresh, dispatch, density toggle, archive toggle, and last-updated time do not jump around during refreshes.
- Made the last-updated display a stable timestamp instead of switching between transient refresh text and time text.
- Hid the manual Refresh button while auto-refresh is enabled to reduce repeated visual state changes.
- Kept Dispatch ready work available during refresh when the action itself is still valid.
- Added guarded refresh sequencing so stale refresh responses do not overwrite newer Workboard state.

### Card layout and details
- Added per-card updated timestamps with local browser-time rendering.
- Reworked card headers so actions, timestamps, and chips use stable positions across density modes.
- Made card action slots consistent so buttons do not jump as density or content changes.
- Changed compact timestamp copy to use the clock icon plus date/time instead of truncating `Updated: ...`.
- Updated the details drawer to show matching date and time precision.
- Made clicking the already-selected card close the details drawer.

### Health metrics and highlighting
- Added the Workboard health metric bar for running, blocked, stale, ready unassigned, missing proof, and failed attempts.
- Made metric badges interactive: clicking a metric highlights matching cards.
- Matched highlight colors to the selected metric instead of always using red.
- Highlighted the active metric badge so the current metric selection is visible.
- Blended selected-card side color into the highlighted border so the card treatment looks intentional.
- Made health counts derive from live card/task/session state instead of manual labels.

### Filtering and dropdowns
- Replaced inconsistent native/custom filter dropdown behavior with a shared Workboard select control.
- Made selecting one dropdown close the previously open dropdown.
- Capitalized priority labels for consistency.
- Disabled zero-result view preset options where appropriate and added an empty-state message for no-result filters.
- Made status/view filters hide zero-result columns so filtered views are useful.
- Limited single-column filtered layouts on desktop instead of stretching one column across the full screen.
- Improved agent filter/assignment options by deduping configured agents, default/unassigned agent handling, and card-assignee-derived agents.

### Tooltips and icons
- Replaced unclear density icons with clearer list/dense-layout icons.
- Added tooltips across Workboard icon buttons.
- Reduced tooltip delay globally for Workboard buttons.
- Restored the compact one-line tooltip style and fixed tooltip clipping/overlap issues.
- Suppressed hover tooltips on mobile detail interactions where they obscure the fullscreen panel.

### Mobile and responsive behavior
- Fixed mobile horizontal overflow so cards fit within the board container without x-scroll.
- Restored card visibility after the mobile column/layout adjustment.
- Made the new-card modal fullscreen on mobile and larger on desktop.
- Fixed mobile dropdown placement inside the new-card modal.
- Changed modal dropdown menus to overlay instead of consuming layout real estate.
- Made mobile details open as a fullscreen panel.

### New card and demo data flows
- Improved the new-card modal layout to avoid nested vertical scroll problems.
- Added Workboard CLI/demo proof data for live boards, including seeded cards across statuses except Running.
- Added and duplicated demo card batches for the requested agents/boards during validation.

### Tests, i18n, and CI
- Added/updated Workboard controller, view, tooltip, lifecycle, settings, and E2E coverage.
- Regenerated Control UI i18n outputs for the new Workboard strings.
- Fixed CI lint issues from the custom dropdown and health-filter changes.
- Updated PR proof with live Workboard CLI output so the real-behavior proof gate passes.

## Verification
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`
- `PATH=/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/workboard.test.ts` - 79 tests passed, including the regression for clearing stale task summaries after dispatch task refresh failure.
- `PATH=/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/workboard.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/workboard.test.ts ui/src/ui/dom-tooltips.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-settings.refresh-active-tab.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-lifecycle.node.test.ts` - 5 files, 184 tests passed.
- `PATH=/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH pnpm ui:i18n:check`
- `PATH=/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH pnpm tsgo:test:ui`
- `PATH=/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check ui/src/ui/controllers/workboard.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/workboard.test.ts`
- `PATH=/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main --codex-bin /Users/jason/.codex/openclaw-pr-autopilot/bin/codex-persist-sessions --model codex=gpt-5.5 --thinking codex=high` - first run found the stale task-summary dispatch issue; after commit `b89813ccc6f6c8517160d65d7ec30bc242cf17df`, rerun was clean with no accepted/actionable findings.
- Current branch proof was run at head `b89813ccc6f6c8517160d65d7ec30bc242cf17df` against current `upstream/main` `9827490f5f735a567716f128ffcdb892d9d39324`.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Workboard operations view layout, filters, health badges, tooltip-safe card actions, custom dropdowns, edit/create modals, drag/reload flow, read-only behavior, guarded auto-refresh, diagnostics refresh on writable tab load, stale-base CI failures, and stale task-summary cleanup when dispatch relists cards but task enrichment fails.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw PR branch at head `b89813ccc6f6c8517160d65d7ec30bc242cf17df`, reviewed and diff-checked against current `upstream/main` `9827490f5f735a567716f128ffcdb892d9d39324`, using the real local Workboard plugin state plus focused deterministic Workboard/UI tests.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```sh
pnpm openclaw --version
pnpm openclaw plugins list --enabled --json | node -e '<summarize enabled Workboard plugin>'
pnpm openclaw workboard list --json | node -e '<summarize live card statuses without printing card contents>'
git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/workboard.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/workboard.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/workboard.test.ts ui/src/ui/dom-tooltips.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-settings.refresh-active-tab.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-lifecycle.node.test.ts
pnpm ui:i18n:check
pnpm tsgo:test:ui
node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check ui/src/ui/controllers/workboard.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/workboard.test.ts
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main --codex-bin /Users/jason/.codex/openclaw-pr-autopilot/bin/codex-persist-sessions --model codex=gpt-5.5 --thinking codex=high
```

Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the real OpenClaw CLI on current head `b89813c`:
```text
$ pnpm openclaw --version
OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (b89813c)

$ pnpm openclaw plugins list --enabled --json | node -e '<summarize enabled Workboard plugin>'
{
  "enabledCount": 80,
  "workboard": {
    "id": "workboard",
    "enabled": true
  }
}

$ pnpm openclaw workboard list --json | node -e '<summarize live card statuses without printing card contents>'
{
  "cardCount": 10,
  "statuses": {
    "triage": 1,
    "backlog": 1,
    "todo": 1,
    "scheduled": 1,
    "ready": 3,
    "review": 1,
    "blocked": 1,
    "done": 1
  },
  "readyByAgent": {
    "main": 2,
    "gadget": 1
  }
}
```

Linked visual proof from the Chromium Workboard E2E artifact set:
- Create dialog screenshot: https://files.catbox.moe/3oczzf.png
- Edited card screenshot: https://files.catbox.moe/4b90q2.png
- Lifecycle review screenshot: https://files.catbox.moe/anspcb.png
- Read-only board screenshot: https://files.catbox.moe/21bnki.png
- Drag/status persistence screenshot: https://files.catbox.moe/ggu4pd.png
- Writable flow recording: https://files.catbox.moe/1asjze.webm

Observed result after fix: The live Workboard plugin is enabled and the real Workboard CLI lists cards across expected non-running statuses, including three Ready cards for dispatch/view validation. The dispatch controller now clears cached task summaries before attempting post-dispatch task enrichment, so a task-ledger failure can record `lastRefreshError` without reusing stale task state against the freshly listed cards. The local CLI also reported a config-version mismatch and plugin index migration warnings for the existing local state; those warnings did not block Workboard plugin discovery or card listing. Focused Workboard tests, UI typecheck, i18n check, formatting check, diff whitespace check, and branch autoreview all pass on pushed head `b89813ccc6f6c8517160d65d7ec30bc242cf17df`.

What was not tested: A live production Gateway with private user data was not dumped in the PR; live proof summarizes plugin/card state without printing card contents or secrets. Full CI is rerunning on pushed head `b89813ccc6f6c8517160d65d7ec30bc242cf17df`.
